sprints.name: 🧭 Platform Simplification — Parallel Explorer Recommendations
sprints.rationale: Six read-only parallel explorers audited the platform for behavior-preserving simplification opportunities: server domain shape, intelligence slices, frontend data flow, stacked feature surfaces, integration adapters, and test/guardrail leverage. This sprint captures those findings as a pick-up-ready backlog. The goal is not to remove functionality or pursue a broad rewrite; it is to make existing platform concepts more canonical by consolidating repeated lifecycle logic, cache invalidation, prompt assembly, provider transport, and guardrail patterns behind the conventions the codebase already uses.
sprints.notes: Source: 2026-06-09 parallel simplification explorer batch. All items are recommendation-only and pending. Before implementing any item: run the normal brainstorm/spec/pre-plan-audit/plan flow where the scope crosses multiple files; preserve behavior first; use phase-per-PR for high-blast-radius work; add parity tests before rewiring user-visible behavior. Recommended first PR: frontend invalidation helper extraction, starting with keyword mutation invalidation bundles before broader WS registry work.

items.title: Frontend cache simplification — extract keyword mutation invalidation bundles
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Frontend Data Flow
items.notes: Recommended first PR. Scope: `src/hooks/admin/useKeywordCommandCenter.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useLocalSeo.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useOutcomes.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useCopyPipeline.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useWorkOrders.ts`, `src/lib/queryKeys.ts`, and existing helper `src/lib/background-job-helpers.ts`. Current complexity: mutations repeat domain cache groups by hand, especially keyword strategy/KCC/rank tracking/intelligence. Behavior-preserving path: add `invalidateMany(queryClient, keys)` plus domain helpers such as `keywordMutationInvalidationKeys(wsId)`, `copyEntryInvalidationKeys(wsId, entryId?)`, `localSeoInvalidationKeys(wsId)`. Start with the KCC/rank-tracking bundle because several mutations share the same invalidation set and recent audit work touched that surface. Do not change event names or query key factories in the first PR. Tests/guardrails: focused QueryClient spy tests proving before/after invalidated keys match; keep `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, and relevant KCC hook/component tests green.

items.title: Frontend cache simplification — central event-to-query invalidation registry
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Frontend Data Flow
items.notes: Scope: `src/hooks/useWsInvalidation.ts`, `src/components/ClientDashboard.tsx`, `src/components/client/inbox/UnifiedInbox.tsx`, `src/components/client/ClientCopyReview.tsx`, `src/components/admin/ClientDeliverablesPane.tsx`, `src/lib/queryKeys.ts`, `src/lib/wsEvents.ts`. Current complexity: many websocket handlers repeat `queryClient.invalidateQueries(...)`; client-specific handlers duplicate parts of the central admin invalidation hook while also mixing local UI side effects. Behavior-preserving path: after domain invalidation bundles exist, add a shared registry like `getWorkspaceInvalidationKeys(event, workspaceId, payload, scope)` and have handlers call `invalidateMany`. Keep UI-only websocket behavior local (progress bars, modal state, optimistic notifications). Migrate event-by-event, starting with high-volume events such as `CONTENT_UPDATED`, `STRATEGY_UPDATED`, `COPY_SECTION_UPDATED`, `DELIVERABLE_UPDATED`, and rank/keyword events. Tests/guardrails: unit coverage for event-to-key mapping, keep `tests/integration/broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts` and `tests/contract/ws-invalidation-coverage.test.ts` green, and add a guardrail that new workspace broadcasts either register invalidation keys or document why they are UI-only.

items.title: Frontend data hooks — create shared GSC base hook mirroring GA4
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Frontend Data Flow
items.notes: Scope: `src/hooks/shared/useGA4Base.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useAdminSearch.ts`, `src/hooks/client/useClientSearch.ts`, `src/api/analytics.ts`, `src/lib/queryKeys.ts`. Current complexity: GA4 has a shared base hook while GSC admin/client hooks repeat parallel `useQuery` setup, loading/error aggregation, and metric naming. Behavior-preserving path: add `src/hooks/shared/useGSCBase.ts` with configurable API calls, key factories, metric labels, and optional admin-only dimensions; keep `useAdminSearch` and `useClientSearch` as thin adapters so component call sites do not change. Tests/guardrails: typecheck, admin/client Search tab smoke render, and assertions that query keys remain `queryKeys.admin.gsc(...)` and `queryKeys.client.gsc(...)` rather than new literals.

items.title: Frontend data flow — move remaining component-local data access into hooks/API wrappers
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Frontend Data Flow
items.notes: Scope: `src/components/client/ClientCopyReview.tsx`, `src/components/client/ContentTab.tsx`, `src/components/client/strategy/useStrategyTrackedKeywords.ts`, `src/components/page-intelligence/usePageIntelligenceKeywordTracking.ts`, `src/hooks/useFeatureFlag.ts`, and matching `src/api/*` wrappers. Current complexity: a few components/hooks still define inline API helpers, direct `fetch()`, or manual loading state instead of React Query and typed API wrappers. Behavior-preserving path: add typed wrappers for public copy review, text exports, feature flags, and tracked keyword reads; move client copy review queries/mutations to `src/hooks/client/`; convert tracked keyword loaders to React Query-backed hooks. Split into two PRs: copy/export wrappers first, tracked keyword hooks second. Tests/guardrails: client copy review interaction tests, strategy/page intelligence tracking smoke tests, and a post-PR `rg "fetch\(" src/components src/hooks` review with intentional exceptions documented.

items.title: Frontend query keys — tighten factory coverage for prefix keys and variants
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Frontend Data Flow
items.notes: Scope: `src/lib/queryKeys.ts`, `src/hooks/useWsInvalidation.ts`, `src/components/ClientDashboard.tsx`, `src/hooks/admin/useOutcomes.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useLocalSeo.ts`. Current complexity: most keys are centralized, but some prefix/variant keys are still assembled ad hoc with array literals/spreads. Behavior-preserving path: add explicit factories such as `admin.blueprintAll(wsId)`, `admin.blueprintVersionsAll(wsId)`, `client.postPreviewAll(wsId)`, `admin.outcomeAction(wsId, actionId)`, `admin.outcomeActionsFiltered(wsId, type, score)`, and `admin.localSeoVariant(wsId, includeSnapshots)`. Migrate one domain at a time. Tests/guardrails: typecheck/build; static grep for `queryKey: [` after migration; consider a pr-check warning that literal query-key arrays require a `querykey-ok` reason unless they are inside `queryKeys.ts`. || DONE 2026-06-09: added exact query-key factories for blueprint prefix invalidation, client post-preview prefix invalidation, outcome action/filter variants, and local SEO summary/snapshot variants. Migrated only the matching listed call sites so cache keys remain byte-for-byte compatible with the former literals/spreads. Added parity coverage in `tests/unit/queryKeys-coverage.test.ts` for legacy key shapes and prefix/detail relationships. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/unit/queryKeys-coverage.test.ts`.

items.title: Integration adapters — introduce a shared Google provider transport wrapper
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Integration Adapters
items.notes: Scope: `server/google-analytics.ts`, `server/search-console.ts`, `server/google-auth.ts`, routes under `server/routes/google.ts`, and Google-related tests. Current complexity: GA4, GSC, and OAuth helpers use separate raw `fetch()` behavior, different timeout handling, and inconsistent provider error envelopes; DataForSEO has stronger centralized transport patterns by comparison. Behavior-preserving path: add `server/google-provider-client.ts` with `googleJson<T>()`, timeout support, sanitized provider error classification, token refresh integration where appropriate, and an explicit degraded/null policy for quota-sensitive reads. Keep existing exported functions and route response shapes stable in the first PR; migrate GA4 read calls first, then GSC query/inspection calls, then OAuth exchange/refresh if it fits. Tests/guardrails: `tests/unit/google-auth.test.ts`, `tests/unit/google-analytics-pure.test.ts`, `tests/unit/gsc-pagination.test.ts`, `tests/integration/google-routes-extended.test.ts`, plus provider-error fixtures for 401/403/429/5xx.

items.title: Integration adapters — standardize Webflow result envelopes and pagination helpers
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Integration Adapters
items.notes: Scope: `server/webflow-client.ts`, `server/webflow-pages.ts`, `server/webflow-cms.ts`, `server/webflow-assets.ts`, Webflow SEO write modules, and Webflow tests. Current complexity: Webflow modules repeat `if (!res.ok) return []|null|{ success:false }`, offset/limit pagination, and stringified status/body errors. Behavior-preserving path: extend `webflow-client.ts` with `webflowJson<T>()`, `webflowMutation()`, `paginateWebflow<T>()`, and a typed `WebflowResult<T>`. Preserve existing product function return shapes as adapters over the helper. Split read-only endpoints first; mutate/write flows second because upload/delete/CMS repair behavior is higher risk. Tests/guardrails: `tests/unit/webflow-client.test.ts`, `tests/unit/webflow-pages-pure.test.ts`, `tests/unit/webflow-assets.test.ts`, `tests/integration/webflow-cms-writes.test.ts`, `tests/integration/webflow-seo-writes.test.ts`; assert current degradation behavior is unchanged.

items.title: Integration adapters — extract DataForSEO operation runner for cache/credits/telemetry
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Integration Adapters
items.notes: Scope: `server/providers/dataforseo-provider.ts`, `server/seo-data-provider.ts`, keyword provider tests, and KCC provider consumers. Current complexity: methods around related keywords, questions, ideas, site keywords, and backlinks repeat cache read/write, credit logging, task-error handling, `areCreditsExhausted()` branches, and empty-result fallback behavior. Behavior-preserving path: introduce `runDataForSeoOperation<T>()` with endpoint, method, cache key/TTL, normalizer, empty fallback, and credit endpoint label. Keep `apiCall`/`apiGet` as thin wrappers or collapse to a method-aware helper after parity tests. Split into request runner first, then cache/credit runner. Tests/guardrails: `tests/unit/dataforseo-provider.test.ts`, `tests/unit/seo-data-provider.test.ts`, `tests/contract/keyword-provider-abstraction.test.ts`, and keyword command center provider coverage for degraded/empty/credit-exhausted responses.

items.title: Integration adapters — extract Stripe checkout route preflight
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Integration Adapters
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/stripe.ts`, `server/stripe.ts`, and Stripe integration tests. Current complexity: create checkout, cart checkout, and upgrade checkout repeat configured checks, workspace lookup, external billing guard, base URL construction, success/cancel URL construction, and sanitized provider error response handling. Behavior-preserving path: add route-local `buildCheckoutContext(req, workspaceId, returnTab)` or `withStripeCheckoutPreflight()` returning `{ ws, baseUrl, successUrl, cancelUrl }`; leave Stripe SDK/session creation in `server/stripe.ts`. Tests/guardrails: `tests/integration/stripe-checkout-flow.test.ts`, `tests/integration/stripe-api.test.ts`, `tests/integration/stripe-admin-auth.test.ts`; verify all existing API error shapes remain `{ error: string }`. || DONE 2026-06-09: added route-local `buildCheckoutContext()` and exported pure `buildCheckoutRedirectUrls()` in `server/routes/stripe.ts`; migrated create-checkout, cart-checkout, and public upgrade-checkout to share workspace lookup, external billing guard, and redirect URL construction while leaving Stripe SDK/session/payment creation in `server/stripe.ts`. Added `tests/unit/stripe-checkout-preflight.test.ts` to pin legacy success/cancel URL parity for content, health, and plans return tabs. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/unit/stripe-checkout-preflight.test.ts tests/integration/stripe-checkout-flow.test.ts tests/integration/stripe-api.test.ts tests/integration/stripe-admin-auth.test.ts`.

items.title: Integration adapters — share AI provider HTTP retry/backoff helper internally
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Integration Adapters
items.notes: Scope: `server/openai-helpers.ts`, `server/anthropic-helpers.ts`, `server/ai.ts`, and AI helper tests. Current complexity: OpenAI and Anthropic helpers duplicate retry-after/backoff/timeout logic; Anthropic tool-use path has a separate retry implementation and less consistent trace/cancellation behavior. Behavior-preserving path: create internal `runAiHttpRequest()` or `withProviderRetry()` used only by provider helpers. Keep `callAI()` as the public boundary and keep provider-specific response normalization separate. Do not expose new direct provider callers. Tests/guardrails: `tests/unit/openai-helpers.test.ts`, `tests/unit/anthropic-helpers.test.ts`, `tests/contract/ai-dispatch-migration.test.ts`, `tests/contract/ai-operation-registry-usage.test.ts`; assert retry timing/error classification parity.

items.title: Intelligence slices — extract optional enrichment/degradation helper
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Intelligence/Slice Complexity
items.notes: Scope: `server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts`, `server/intelligence/client-signals-slice.ts`, `server/intelligence/operational-slice.ts`, `server/intelligence/content-pipeline-slice.ts`, `server/intelligence/site-health-slice.ts`, plus slice tests. Current complexity: assemblers repeat many `try { dynamic import/read/map } catch { log; fallback }` blocks, making degradation rules verbose and easy to drift. Behavior-preserving path: add a small helper like `readOptionalSlicePart(label, workspaceId, fallback, fn, { warnProgrammingErrors })`; migrate one slice at a time, starting with `operational` or `contentPipeline`. Keep slice-specific mapping logic local. Tests/guardrails: assembler degradation tests where one dependency throws, seeded workspace output snapshots, and logging assertions that expected optional failures remain warnings while programming errors still surface where required.

items.title: Intelligence prompt builders — add lightweight SEO prompt context builder
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Intelligence/Slice Complexity
items.notes: Scope: `server/intelligence/generation-context-builders.ts`, `server/content-posts-ai.ts`, `server/page-analysis-job.ts`, `server/routes/public-analytics.ts`, `server/routes/google.ts`, `server/webflow-seo-bulk-analyze-job.ts`. Current complexity: several consumers manually assemble the same `seoContext` + `learnings` + page-map prompt blocks. Behavior-preserving path: add `buildSeoPromptContext()` beside existing generation context builders with options for `includePageMap`, `learningsDomain`, `verbosity`, `tokenBudget`, and optional backlink enrichment. Prove output parity against one current manual composition before migrating call sites. Tests/guardrails: unit test for builder output equality, prompt snapshot for a seeded workspace, and a future pr-check warning for new manual `formatForPrompt(...seoContext/learnings...) + formatPageMapForPrompt` compositions.

items.title: Intelligence prompt builders — centralize SEO sub-block composition for assists/jobs
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Intelligence/Slice Complexity
items.notes: Scope: `server/intelligence/page-assist-context-builder.ts`, `server/copy-generation.ts`, `server/routes/jobs.ts`, `server/internal-links.ts`, and SEO context tests. Current complexity: consumers manually stitch `formatKeywordsForPrompt`, `effectiveBrandVoiceBlock`, personas, knowledge base, and page-map blocks, sometimes with page-keyword overrides. Behavior-preserving path: export typed `buildSeoPromptBlocks(seoContext, opts)` returning `{ keywordBlock, brandVoiceBlock, personasBlock, knowledgeBlock, pageMapBlock }`; use it in `buildPageAssistContext()` first, then migrate jobs/copy/internal-linking. Tests/guardrails: calibrated voice-profile regression ensuring no raw `brandVoice` bypasses authority resolution; output parity tests for page keyword override and knowledge/persona formatting.

items.title: Intelligence prompt formatting — split slice formatters behind existing facade
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Intelligence/Slice Complexity
items.notes: Scope: `server/intelligence/formatters.ts` and future `server/intelligence/formatters/<slice>.ts` files. Current complexity: one large formatter owns dispatch, cold-start behavior, token budget, and every slice renderer. Behavior-preserving path: keep `formatForPrompt()` as the only public facade and move two or three low-risk section renderers into slice-owned formatter modules first. Introduce a typed formatter registry only after snapshots prove output parity. Tests/guardrails: golden prompt snapshots for compact/standard/detailed verbosity, token-budget tests, cold-start tests, and no prompt text changes unless explicitly approved.

items.title: Intelligence facade — introduce typed slice metadata registry incrementally
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Intelligence/Slice Complexity
items.notes: Scope: `server/workspace-intelligence.ts`, `shared/types/intelligence.ts`, `server/intelligence/formatters.ts`, slice assembler imports. Current complexity: slice names, prompt-formattable status, required options, assembly dispatch, and formatting order are spread across arrays and switches. Behavior-preserving path: add a server-side registry with assembler, prompt formatter metadata, `promptFormattable`, `requires.pagePath/siteId`, and timeout. Keep shared consts as the public type source initially; start with assembly dispatch only, then formatter dispatch later. Tests/guardrails: contract test that every `INTELLIGENCE_SLICES` value has a registry entry; TypeScript should fail on missing assembler metadata; no call-site behavior changes in first PR.

items.title: Server domain shape — extract approval batch lifecycle services from thick route code
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/approvals.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-respond.ts`, approval stores, page-state sync, activity, broadcasts, and Webflow apply behavior. Current complexity: approval create/respond/apply spreads page status derivation, activity, team email, Webflow writes, outcome tracking, recommendation resolution, and deliverable mirror sync across route code and domain modules. Behavior-preserving path: extract `approval-batch-mutations.ts` and `approval-batch-apply.ts`; routes become thin adapters calling `createApprovalBatchForClient`, `respondToApprovalItem`, and `applyApprovedBatchItems`. Split into two PRs: create/respond first, apply/Webflow side effects second. Tests/guardrails: approval create/respond/apply integration tests, partial apply retry tests, unified inbox respond parity, page-state aggregation contract, and broadcast assertions.

items.title: Server domain shape — unify schema plan lifecycle mutations
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/webflow-schema.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/schema-plan-dual-write.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/schema-plan-respond.ts`, schema plan store/job interactions, broadcasts, deliverables. Current complexity: plan role updates, send-to-client, activation, delete, conflict checks, feedback handling, activity, intelligence invalidation, and deliverable sync are repeated around route handlers. Behavior-preserving path: add `server/domains/schema/schema-plan-lifecycle.ts` with `updatePlanRoles`, `sendSchemaPlanToClient`, `activateSchemaPlan`, `deleteSchemaPlanForSite`, and `respondToSchemaPlan`. Keep existing routes and response shapes. Tests/guardrails: schema plan send/feedback/delete integration tests, active-job conflict contract, deliverable sync event assertions, and state transition validation.

items.title: Server jobs — move legacy inline `/api/jobs` bodies into runner modules
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/jobs.ts`, newer runner pattern in `server/routes/webflow-seo-jobs.ts`, and job modules for seo-audit, image compression, bulk alt text, bulk SEO fixes, and sales reports. Current complexity: `/api/jobs` mixes validation, job creation, dispatch, long-running execution, result shaping, and cancellation semantics; newer jobs already use dedicated runner modules. Behavior-preserving path: introduce a small job-handler registry and move legacy bodies into `*-job.ts` modules by job family. Keep `/api/jobs` as auth/validation/dispatch adapter. Split into 3-5 small PRs; do not migrate all job types at once. Tests/guardrails: route start tests per job type, active-job conflict tests, cancellation semantics where cancellable, result shape snapshots, `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES` lifecycle contract coverage.

items.title: Server Webflow assets — extract shared image optimization service
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/webflow-alt-text.ts`, `server/routes/jobs.ts`, Webflow asset/CMS modules, `repairCmsReferences`, image compression/upload/delete flows. Current complexity: SVG/JPEG/PNG/WebP branches, temp-file handling, upload/delete behavior, and CMS reference repair are duplicated between route and job code; bulk-compress currently calls an internal HTTP route instead of the domain function directly. Behavior-preserving path: extract `server/domains/webflow-assets/image-optimization.ts` with `compressImageBuffer`, `replaceCompressedAsset`, and `repairCmsReferences`; route and jobs call the service directly. Tests/guardrails: unit tests for extension handling and skip thresholds; integration-style mocks for upload failure, CMS repair failure, and old asset preservation.

items.title: Server content plan — extract review/send-to-client mutations from route code
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/content-plan-review.ts`, approval batch construction, deliverable mirrors, matrix cell status changes, broadcasts, and activity logging. Current complexity: content-plan send-to-client behavior hand-builds approval batches and mirrors deliverables inline. Behavior-preserving path: extract `server/domains/content-plan/review-mutations.ts` with `sendTemplateForReview`, `sendSamplesForReview`, `flagMatrixCell`, and `batchApproveMatrixCells`; keep route serializers nearby or in a serializer module. Tests/guardrails: content plan review route tests, approval-batch payload shape tests, cell status transition assertions, and broadcast event contract.

items.title: Feature stack — finish one admin keyword operating surface
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `src/components/KeywordHub.tsx`, `src/components/KeywordCommandCenter.tsx`, `src/components/RankTracker.tsx`, `src/hooks/admin/useKeywordHubState.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useKeywordCommandCenter.ts`, `server/keyword-command-center.ts`, `server/routes/keyword-command-center.ts`, `server/routes/rank-tracking.ts`, `server/rank-tracking.ts`. User-facing behavior to preserve: keyword lifecycle actions, rank history, snapshot capture, pinned trends, strategy feedback, View-in-Hub deep links, and local SEO segments. Current complexity: Keyword Hub and legacy KeywordCommandCenter both own filtering/search/deep-link/drawer/bulk action concerns; RankTracker and strategy surfaces also perform direct tracking mutations and cache invalidation. Behavior-preserving path: make `KeywordHub` the sole admin keyword lifecycle shell; keep `RankTracker` measurement-only, embedded or linked from the Hub drawer; extract shared rank tracking mutations/invalidation first; retire `KeywordCommandCenter.tsx` only after flag parity. Tests/guardrails: `KeywordHub.test.tsx`, `KeywordCommandCenterDeepLink.test.tsx`, `RankTracker.test.tsx`, rank/KCC route tests, and a contract that `RANK_TRACKING_UPDATED` invalidates Hub, latest/history ranks, strategy, and client rank reads.

items.title: Feature stack — complete ClientDeliverable spine and shrink legacy inbox adapters
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `src/components/client/InboxTab.tsx`, `src/components/client/inbox/UnifiedInbox.tsx`, `src/lib/decision-adapters.ts`, `src/hooks/client/useUnifiedInbox.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/unified-inbox-read.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/send-to-client.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/deliverable-adapters/*`, `server/routes/deliverables.ts`, legacy approval/client-action/public-content routes. User-facing behavior to preserve: Decisions/Reviews/Conversations routing, note-based conversations, inline single-action approval, bulk modal, per-item edits, apply-to-website, projected copy/content reviews, requests, and work-order track lane. Current complexity: the canonical deliverable path exists, but legacy ApprovalBatch/ClientAction normalizers and source-specific public response/apply paths still leak into client rendering and props. Behavior-preserving path: move section routing and display count logic to a single deliverable projection utility; keep source-specific writes behind deliverable adapters; reduce `InboxTab` props to what `UnifiedInbox` needs; leave legacy routes available for compatibility. Tests/guardrails: unified inbox read route, deliverables route, decision adapters, InboxTab unified component coverage, approval dual-write tests, edited item propagation, and public approval broadcast tests.

items.title: Feature stack — consolidate client-facing insight projections into a read-only view model

items.title: Feature stack — extract content pipeline workflow mutations into focused hooks/services
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx`, `src/components/ContentBriefs.tsx`, `src/components/ContentManager.tsx`, `src/hooks/admin/useAdminBriefs.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useAdminPosts.ts`, `src/api/content.ts`, `server/routes/content-briefs.ts`, `server/routes/content-requests.ts`, `server/routes/content-posts.ts`, `server/domains/content/send-post-to-client.ts`, `server/content-brief.ts`, `server/content-posts.ts`, `server/content-requests.ts`. Current complexity: large components still mix workflow mutation logic, cache writes, raw API calls, local state machines, and rendering. Behavior-preserving path: create focused hooks such as `useAdminBriefWorkflow`, `useAdminContentRequestWorkflow`, and `useAdminPostWorkflow` over `src/api/content.ts`; keep components as render/state shells. Server route growth should stop; reusable transitions move into content-domain services. Tests/guardrails: ContentBriefs component tests, ContentManager send-to-client tests, content brief/post/request route tests, status transition tests, and public post review broadcast tests.

items.title: Feature stack — centralize keyword opportunity display projection without merging scoring layers
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts`, `server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts`, `server/keyword-command-center.ts`, `server/keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts`, `server/keyword-recommendations.ts`, `server/briefing-client-projection.ts`, `src/components/client/strategy/StrategyKeywordDrawer.tsx`, `src/components/keyword-command-center/KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx`, `src/components/client/strategy/StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection.tsx`. User-facing behavior to preserve: Layer 1 Hub keyword value, Layer 2 recommendation opportunity value, client-safe explanations, content-gap ordering, and brief/request flows. Current complexity: scoring layers are intentionally separate, but display/ranking projections are scattered; surfaces consume `valueScore`, `opportunityScore`, fallback ordering, and 'why this matters' explanations differently. Behavior-preserving path: do not merge scoring algorithms. Add a shared projection helper for keyword-bearing opportunities that returns normalized keyword, display score, reasons, dollar value if allowed, client-safe explanation, and sort key. Use behind existing flags where ordering changes. Tests/guardrails: keyword value score tests, opportunity value tests, content gap scoring tests, keyword recommendation ranking tests, client strategy content gap tests, briefing preview tests, and flag-off parity tests.

items.title: Test guardrails — add shared source-contract helper for string-based wiring tests
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/bridge-wiring.test.ts`, `tests/bridges-simple.test.ts`, `tests/cache-invalidation-cluster.test.ts`, and other source-sniffing tests with `readFile-ok` comments. Current complexity: many tests repeat `readFileSync`, bounded section extraction, and `toContain/not.toContain` assertions, spreading platform intent across noisy implementation details. Behavior-preserving path: add `tests/helpers/source-contracts.ts` with `readProjectFile`, `boundedSection`, `expectContainsAll`, and `expectOmitsAll`; migrate bridge/cache tests first. Tests/guardrails: run the migrated tests plus `npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts`; keep helper additive so existing source-contract tests can migrate gradually. || DONE 2026-06-09: added `tests/helpers/source-contracts.ts` with `readProjectFile`, `boundedSection`, `expectContainsAll`, and `expectOmitsAll`; migrated the bridge/cache source-contract cluster (`tests/bridge-wiring.test.ts`, `tests/bridges-simple.test.ts`, `tests/cache-invalidation-cluster.test.ts`) without changing assertions. Remaining source-sniffing tests can migrate opportunistically when adjacent guardrails are touched.

items.title: Test guardrails — extract one WS/event contract parser for coverage tests and pr-check
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/integration/broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts`, `tests/contract/ws-invalidation-coverage.test.ts`, `scripts/pr-check.ts`, and WS event constants. Current complexity: duplicate parsers collect `WS_EVENTS`, broadcasts, frontend handlers, and exemption lists; this overlaps directly with the frontend invalidation registry opportunity. Behavior-preserving path: extract `tests/helpers/ws-contracts.ts` with `parseWsEvents`, `collectBroadcasts`, and `collectHandlers`; let both tests consume the helper. Later extend it with an event-to-query-key expectation table once the frontend registry exists. Tests/guardrails: run broadcast-handler pairs, ws invalidation coverage, and pr-check tests; ensure CSS class/string false positives remain excluded. || DONE 2026-06-09: extracted shared WS contract parser logic into `scripts/ws-contract-parser.ts` so both static tests and `scripts/pr-check.ts` use the same `WS_EVENTS`, centralized handler, broadcast, and frontend subscription parsing. Migrated `broadcast-handler-pairs`, `ws-invalidation-coverage`, and pr-check's WS event loader/centralization rule without changing exemptions or event semantics. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/integration/broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts tests/contract/ws-invalidation-coverage.test.ts tests/pr-check.test.ts`.

items.title: Test infrastructure — simplify integration server port ownership
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/integration/helpers.ts`, `tests/meta-port-uniqueness.test.ts`, `scripts/pr-check.ts`, and integration tests using `createTestContext(PORT)`. Current complexity: many tests allocate literal ports, requiring meta-test and pr-check enforcement plus `port-ok` comments. Behavior-preserving path: introduce `createEphemeralTestContext()` or `tests/helpers/ports.ts` allocator while keeping literal support during migration. First PR should only add the helper and migrate one representative integration file; broad migration can be opportunistic. Tests/guardrails: `tests/meta-port-uniqueness.test.ts`, a representative integration test, and smoke tests; ensure parallel Vitest behavior remains stable.

items.title: Test guardrails — table-drive background job lifecycle coverage
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/contract/background-job-coverage-contract.test.ts`, `shared/types/background-jobs.ts`, and integration tests around job mutation safety/lifecycle. Current complexity: manual `COVERAGE_SIGNALS` and repeated assertions for job type/status/progress/message/result behavior. Behavior-preserving path: add a test-only registry mapping each `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.*` entry to lifecycle expectations and signal files; use table-driven assertions. Tests/guardrails: background job coverage contract, lifecycle integration tests, and feature flag/job type verification; keep `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES` as the product authority.

items.title: Test guardrails — add pr-check fixture DSL and generated docs drift test
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/pr-check.test.ts`, `scripts/pr-check.ts`, `scripts/generate-rules-doc.ts`, `docs/rules/automated-rules.md`. Current complexity: pr-check tests are large and noisy, and docs drift is verified manually through `npm run rules:generate`. Behavior-preserving path: add an `expectRuleCases(rule, cases)` fixture DSL for compact positive/negative cases; add a contract test that renders generated rules content and compares to committed `automated-rules.md` without writing. Tests/guardrails: `npx vitest run tests/pr-check.test.ts`, `npm run rules:generate`, and `git diff --exit-code docs/rules/automated-rules.md`. || DONE 2026-06-09: exported `renderAutomatedRulesDoc()` from `scripts/generate-rules-doc.ts` while preserving CLI generation, added a no-write docs drift test against committed `docs/rules/automated-rules.md`, and introduced `expectRuleCases()` in `tests/pr-check.test.ts` with the Global keydown customCheck block migrated as the first representative fixture table. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/pr-check.test.ts`; `npm run rules:generate && git diff --exit-code docs/rules/automated-rules.md`.

sprints.name: 🔑 Keyword Hub (Wave 4) — KCC + Rank Tracker Consolidation
sprints.rationale: Consolidate the admin Keyword Command Center (seo-keywords) and the standalone Rank Tracker (seo-ranks) into one keyword-first Hub so a user can follow any keyword's full journey — strategy gap → tracking decision → live national + per-market rank → why retired — in one admin surface, with keyword identity carried in every cross-surface URL. The visible payoff of the Wave 1–3 data-model consolidation.
sprints.notes: Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-keyword-hub-design.md (approved). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-04-keyword-hub.md. Six sequential flag-gated phases (P0–P5), each its own PR merged + green on staging before the next. Dark behind the keyword-hub flag through P4; visible-to-users flip gated on owner staging sign-off at P5.

items.title: Keyword Hub (Wave 4) — unified keyword surface behind the keyword-hub flag (P0–P5)
items.notes: P0 (shell + flag + table migration) DARK: keyword-hub flag added (default false), strategyOwned exposed on the KCC tracking row (additive, admin-only, never on the public read path), and the bespoke KCC KeywordRow grid + hand-rolled RankTracker grid migrated onto the canonical KeywordTable primitive (behavior-preserving). Flag OFF — zero user-visible change. P1–P5 pending (phase-per-PR).

items.title: Keyword Universe Overhaul — full coverage (ranking + discovery), junk gate, sort, window, normalization
items.notes: Phase-per-PR. Task 0 (shared contracts: sort enum +clicks/difficulty, rows direction param, RowCandidateKey clicks/difficulty, isJunkKeywordString Tier-1 gate, GSC_METRIC_WINDOW_DAYS constant, keyword-universe-full flag) done. Tasks 1 sort / 2 junk gate / 3 uncap (flag-gated) / 4 window+banner / 5 normalizer pending.

items.title: Keyword Value Scoring — value-first, posture-driven opportunity score (Hub sort + content-gap spine)
items.notes: Phase 0 (flag + pure scoring module + types) done. Phase 1 (Hub wiring: intent resolution, ScoringContext, precomputed valueScore, flag-selected accessor, parity probe) done. Phase 2 (content-gap opportunity spine swap) done. All shipped DARK behind keyword-value-scoring flag (default OFF, byte-identical when OFF). Owner-gated: flip per-workspace on staging to verify value-first ordering (transactional local leads, national-informational demoted under local posture), then merge staging→main.

sprints.name: 🎯 Keyword Value — propagation, money, GSC signals, client display
sprints.rationale: Phase 0–2 of keyword-value-scoring shipped the value-first score into the Hub sort + content-gap spine, but a wiring audit found the new score is still siloed: it does NOT propagate to briefs / content-gen / titles-metas / schema / internal-linking / content-plan / AI-context, and the client briefing actively demotes it (sorts by raw volume). This sprint propagates ONE consistent value score everywhere, turns it into dollars, surfaces the per-keyword GSC data already collected-but-discarded (striking-distance, momentum), and builds the client-facing layer — all additive, reusing signals already in hand, no new data collection.
sprints.notes: Sequenced by effort-to-value with dependencies (item titles tagged Wave 1–5). Wave 1 makes the score honest+legible+consistent (foundation, unblocks the client display); Wave 2 = dollars; Wave 3 = surface discarded GSC signals (one per-keyword join → 3 surfaces); Wave 4 = the client keyword display layer; Wave 5 = opportunistic/deeper. Each item runs the established brainstorm→spec→plan→phased-PR flow and is flag-gated where it changes ranking/output. Audit source: 2026-06-05 conversation (10-agent wiring audit + diverse-lens ideation). || 2026-06-05 ANTI-DUPLICATION PLACEMENT AUDIT (6-surface inventory): each item annotated with its canonical HOME + verdict (exists / fold-into / new). DROPPED kwv-client-wins-this-month — the named rank-movement win already ships end-to-end (ranking-mover.ts -> signal-story-registry.ts:410 -> InsightsBriefingPage DataSpread); its only real need (value-rank which named win leads) merged into kwv-one-score-everywhere. ADDED kwv-titles-metas-schema-priority + kwv-internal-linking-value-priority to capture wiring-audit surfaces not yet tracked. Rule: enrich existing Insights/Wins/Recommendations/ROI/StrategyKeywordDrawer/PageKeywordMapContent surfaces — NO new client keyword tab, NO second dollar engine. KEYSTONE = kwv-one-score-everywhere (reconcile keyword-value-score vs opportunity-value into one source of truth) — brainstorm first.

items.title: Value-score breakdown — return + label the components instead of a bare 0-100
items.notes: Wave 1 (foundation). computeKeywordValueScore already computes commercialValue (intent × CPC), demand, winnability, and the local multiplier internally, then discards them. Return + label them so the Hub/brief/client show WHY a low-volume keyword outranks a high-volume one ('commercial intent, $9 CPC, winnable KD 24, local 1.5×'). Unblocks the client display (kwv-client-why-worth-it) and richer briefs. No new data. || PLACEMENT [exists-pattern]: OpportunityScore.components (buildComponents) already computes+renders this client-side ('Why this is #1' OverviewTab, StrategyKeywordDrawer 'See the numbers'). Work = expose the SAME components on the Hub valueScore (drawer), not a new breakdown. Depends on the score-consolidation decision (kwv-one-score-everywhere). || DONE 2026-06-05 (PR 2, branch feat/kwv-value-breakdown): keywordValueReasons helper (server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts) computed server-side, serialized as valueReasons onto the KCC row (finalizeDraftRow) + the client strategy explanation (keyword-strategy-ux.ts), rendered in both drawers (StrategyKeywordDrawer 'See the numbers' + KeywordDetailDrawer 'Why this score', blue=data). Flag-gated on keyword-value-scoring.

items.title: Feed real CPC into the content-gap value score (fix the cpc:undefined hard-pass)
items.notes: Wave 1. keyword-strategy-enrichment hard-passes cpc:undefined into computeKeywordValueScore, so every content-gap value score uses the CPC_UNKNOWN=0.5 mid-band proxy instead of real money. ContentGap + the matched pageMap entry already carry cpc. Pass it through so commercial gaps reflect commercial value. Correctness fix that makes the score honest before propagating it. || PLACEMENT [new, ~1-line]: populate OpportunityInput.cpc for the content_gap branch (cpc plumbing already exists in opportunity-value.ts/roi.ts). No surface. || DONE 2026-06-05 (PR 1, branch feat/keyword-value-legible-dollarized): content_gaps.cpc column + ContentGap/StrategyContentGap types + content-gaps.ts mapper + enrichment populate (domain-hit + API paths) + pass cpc into the value scorer, all in one lockstep commit. cpc is score-only (intentionally NOT raw-exposed on the public content-gap projection).

items.title: One value score everywhere — unify ranking across content-plan, titles/metas, briefs, and the client briefing
items.notes: Wave 1. Four surfaces rank the SAME keywords four contradictory ways: Hub uses valueScore, content-plan uses legacy opportunityScore (keyword-recommendations.ts:159), the client briefing sorts by raw VOLUME buckets (briefing-client-projection.ts:81, demoting high-value low-volume gaps), titles/metas lead by strategy slot order. Make them all rank by the single Hub valueScore. HIGHEST-PRIORITY — fixes the client briefing sort, the exact surface the client keyword display sits on. Flag-gated where it changes ranking. || PLACEMENT [KEYSTONE — architecture decision, brainstorm first]: reconcile the new keyword-value-score vs the existing opportunity-value OV model (two value models today — Hub valueScore is a bare number; OV exposes components + is what client surfaces render). Decide ONE source of truth, then unify ranking across content-plan (keyword-recommendations.ts:159), titles/metas, briefs, client-briefing (fix the volume-bucket sort briefing-client-projection.ts:81), internal-linking. ALSO absorbs the only real need of the DROPPED kwv-client-wins-this-month: re-rank named rank-movement wins on InsightsBriefingPage so the most valuable named mover leads. || KEYSTONE FOUNDATION DONE (PR 1 + PR 2, 2026-06-05): one classifier (deriveValueIntent — shared by both layers; comparison-intent drift closed), layered contract documented (Layer 1 = computeKeywordValueScore / Layer 2 = computeOpportunityValue; surface ownership rule), and KeywordValueComponents interface exposed via computeKeywordValueComponents (commercialValue/demand/winnability/localMultiplier/intent). Signal-gate parity locked by test. 4 scalar callers byte-identical. Surface re-ranking (content-plan/briefs/client-briefing) remains this item's own work.

items.title: Dollar value per keyword + portfolio 'revenue at stake' rollup
items.notes: Wave 2 (money; biggest client wow). Per-keyword '#7 → page-1 ≈ +$340/mo' = CTR-curve uplift (ctr-curve.ts) × monthly volume × valuePerClick (cpc × INTENT_WEIGHT, opportunity-value.ts). Plus a portfolio headline 'close your top N gaps ≈ $X/mo (~$Y/yr ad-equivalent)' reusing roi.ts adSpendEquivalent. Pure reuse of the existing EMV math + CTR curve — no GA4/provider call. Surfaces on Hub, briefs, client briefing, AI context. || PLACEMENT [mostly exists]: ROIDashboard/computeROI is the ONE client $ engine (organicTrafficValue, adSpendEquivalent, per-page trafficValue/mo). DO NOT build a second dollar engine. NEW = only a per-KEYWORD $/mo line (StrategyKeywordDrawer + a Hub $ column) reusing roi.ts avgCPC + the OV CTR-uplift. Watch the contradictory-$ trap next to milestone-attribution.ts. || DONE 2026-06-05 (PR 3, branch feat/kwv-dollar-value): ONE keywordDollarValue helper (server/scoring/keyword-value-money.ts) — currentMonthly = clicks×cpc (equivalence test: == roi.ts trafficValue), upsideMonthly = impressions × ctrAt-uplift × cpc (realized $, NO intentWeight). cpc joined from page_keywords onto KCC metrics.cpc + strategy rows. Per-keyword 'Revenue potential' $ on both drawers (emerald). Portfolio revenueAtStake (Σ upsideMonthly) as a 4th ROIDashboard StatCard. No second $ engine, no new endpoint (rides /api/public/roi + the strategy serialization).

items.title: Per-keyword GSC breakdown in the AI/seoContext slice (named movers, not counts)
items.notes: Wave 3 (enabling join). seo-context-slice reads full per-keyword GSC (getLatestRanks: position/change/clicks/impressions) then collapses to 'N improved, M declined' before any prompt. Keep a top-N per-keyword breakdown (keyword, position, change, clicks, impressions, valueScore) on RankTrackingSummary so AdminChat + generation-context builders can NAME which keyword moved/gained clicks. Foundation for striking-distance + momentum. || PLACEMENT [new slice enrichment]: seoContext/RankTrackingSummary AI-context (named per-keyword movers into prompts). ranking_mover is already in the insights slice for AI — wire from existing data, keep top-N named breakdown.

items.title: Striking-distance keywords — the page-2 nudge list (positions 11-20, value-ranked)
items.notes: Wave 3. 'You have 14 keywords ranking #11-20 — one push moves them to page 1.' Highest-conviction SEO move; nothing names them today (data in getLatestRanks, value via in-hand valueScore). Surface in Hub, recommendations (which mint ZERO recs from rank_tracking today), and the client briefing. Distinct from the GSC-query-derived rankingOpportunities (this is rank_tracking-backed + value-ranked, survives into recs + client). || PLACEMENT [exists]: ranking_opportunity insight (pos 4-20, value-ranked) + SearchTab 'Low-Hanging Fruit' + PageKeywordMap 'Ranking' filter already cover this. NEW = at most a position-band (#11-20) filter/view in the admin Hub (none today). Reuse the insight; relabel/filter, do not add an InsightType.

items.title: Named 28-day momentum + auto rank-gain/rank-loss recommendations
items.notes: Wave 3. Named risers/fallers ('emergency dentist #19→#8 +340 impr; [service] near me #4→#9 — defend it') from getLatestRanks change + getRankHistory 90-day slope. AND mint defend/push recommendations from tracked movement — the rec surface is 100% blind to rank movement today (ranking_mover insight exists but is never converted to a Recommendation). Reuses kwv-gsc-per-keyword-context. || PLACEMENT [split]: named risers/fallers ALREADY ship — ranking_mover insight + ranking-mover.ts client win story (signal-story-registry.ts:410 -> InsightsBriefingPage). The genuinely-NEW half = defend/push RECOMMENDATIONS: add a rank/defend/push RecType + RecActionType + RecSourceCategory via the four-line contract in recommendations.ts (overlaps the existing strategy:ranking-opportunity source). Do NOT re-render named movers as a new surface.

items.title: Client: plain-language 'why this keyword is worth it' badges
items.notes: Wave 4 (client display). Turn the opaque 0-100 into a human reason: 'High-value: people searching this are ready to buy (commercial intent), it's local to your area, and it's winnable — competitors aren't strong here.' Reuses kwv-value-breakdown + ScoringContext posture/city/state already computed per request. Build alongside the client keyword display. || PLACEMENT [exists]: StrategyKeywordDrawer 'Why it's in the strategy' + buildOvGainString phrases + 'Why this is #1' bars already render plain-language reasons. Fold reason badges into those existing rows (and OutcomeWinEntry/ranking-mover narratives), not a new card.

items.title: Client: 'You rank for X but you're missing Y' per-page cards
items.notes: Wave 4 (client display). Per important page: 'Your Teeth Whitening page ranks #4 for "teeth whitening" (320 visits/mo) — but you're invisible for "teeth whitening cost", which 1,200 people search every month.' Joins persisted PageKeywordMap primaryKeyword+position+clicks with the per-page ContentGap the page is NOT ranking for. Both sides exist; never paired per-page on a client surface. || PLACEMENT [fold-in]: 'rank for X' already in PageKeywordMapContent (per-page primary kw + GSC rank/impr/clicks); 'missing Y' is the existing competitor_gap insight. Add a per-page gap badge/row INSIDE PageKeywordMapContent, do not build a new client view.

items.title: Portfolio intent mix + branded-vs-non-branded split
items.notes: Wave 5 (framing). '82% of your targeted keywords are informational, only 6% commercial — that's why traffic is up but leads aren't.' + 'Branded clicks are 68%; non-branded (new demand) is 32% and flat.' Buckets existing per-keyword intent (deriveValueIntent adapter) + partitions existing GSC clicks/impr by brand-name regex. Zero new data; agency-report staples. || PLACEMENT [rollup new on Hub]: per-keyword intent already renders in 3 client places + isBrandedQuery branded-detection already exists in analytics-intelligence. NEW = only the workspace-level PORTFOLIO rollup (intent-mix + branded share) as a Hub header strip — not a per-page card/insight.

items.title: Briefs: surface per-page intent/volume/KD/CPC + thread topic→page into MCP brief context
items.notes: Wave 5. formatPageMapForPrompt emits only keyword strings, dropping each page's searchIntent/volume/difficulty/cpc; render the metrics already on each entry so the brief can lead with the highest-value term. AND prepare_brief_context (MCP) threads no pagePath/targetKeyword, so the external brief model never gets the per-page match — pass topic→targetKeyword through the existing builder. || PLACEMENT [new, brief-side wiring]: content-brief.ts formatPageMapForPrompt is lossy (drops per-page searchIntent/volume/KD/CPC) + MCP prepare_brief_context threads no topic->page. Render the metrics already on each entry + thread the page; also surface the value score as first-class (today only laundered through opportunityScore). NOTE: content/post GENERATION inherits keyword targeting via the brief, so wiring the brief covers generation — no separate content-gen item needed.

items.title: Cannibalization: weight severity by which page actually wins clicks
items.notes: Wave 5. Instead of a generic 'medium' conflict: 'Page A (#4, 120 clicks) and Page B (#9, 8 clicks) compete for "invisalign cost" — consolidate into A.' cannibalization-detection sets severity from structural type only, ignoring rank; join the competing pages' existing tracked ranks/clicks (getLatestRanks) to pick the winner. || PLACEMENT [fold-in]: enrich the EXISTING cannibalization insight (+clickWinnerPage / per-page clicks field, weight selection in computeCannibalizationInsights, surface in the existing projector). Not a new surface.

items.title: Conversion-grounded value-per-click — replace the CPC proxy with what the site actually converts
items.notes: Wave 5 (deepest money win; after kwv-dollar-value proves out). Join GA4 getGA4LandingPages conversions/sessions per page (already an insight) with the page's keywords via GSC QueryPageRow, so a low-CPC-but-high-converting keyword outranks a flashy high-CPC one that never converts. Uplifts opportunity-value.ts valuePerClick and fills the estimatedRevenue:null stub (analytics-intelligence.ts:423). || PLACEMENT [fold-in / planned]: enrich the EXISTING conversion_attribution insight AND take the reserved 'P6' swap point already commented in opportunity-value.ts:370-376 (valuePerClick CPC-proxy -> GA4 estimatedRevenue). A scoring swap behind existing surfaces, not a new VPC insight.

items.title: Titles/metas/schema: lead with the highest-value keyword + revive the dead schema searchIntent field
items.notes: Wave 5 (wiring-audit gap). Title/meta + schema generation ARE keyword-targeted per page but get NO prioritization signal — they lead by strategy slot order, not value, and SchemaContext.searchIntent is declared (schema-suggester.ts:314) but never populated or consumed (dead field). Make seo-copy + buildSchemaContext lead with the highest-value target keyword and use intent to shape schema (Article vs Service/FAQ). HOME: existing seo-copy + buildSchemaContext (server/helpers.ts). PLACEMENT [enrich existing generation path].

items.title: Internal linking: prioritize equity toward high-value / under-optimized pages
items.notes: Wave 5 (wiring-audit gap). Internal-linking prioritization is topicCluster-only and ignores per-page value, optimizationScore, and tracked rank — so it can't steer linking equity to high-value or weak pages. Join the existing valueScore + PageKeywordMap.optimizationScore + tracked rank to weight link suggestions. HOME: existing internal-links module. PLACEMENT [enrich existing module].

sprints.name: 📬 Unified Send-to-Client — Spine + Strangler-Fig Migration
sprints.rationale: Replace the five bespoke 'send to client' pipelines with one ClientDeliverable model + one sendToClient() service + one client inbox + one admin inbox, via a per-type strangler-fig cutover production never feels. Adding a reviewable work type later becomes an adapter, not a sixth subsystem.
sprints.notes: Design: docs/designs/2026-06-01-unified-send-to-client-design.md. Pre-plan audit: docs/superpowers/audits/2026-06-01-unified-send-to-client-audit.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-01-unified-send-to-client.md. [UPDATE 2026-06-04: the temporary rollout flags (`new-inbox-ia`, `unified-inbox`, and the three `unified-deliverables-*` phase-group flags) are retired. The unified inbox and deliverables surfaces are now canonical runtime behavior.]

items.title: Phase 0 — shared contracts (migrations, types, store, state machines, adapter registry, service, route, flags, pr-check rules) DARK

Verified by roadmap audit.]
