items.notes: Migrate each work type onto the spine one PR at a time, gated behind its phase-group flag. Approval-batch family first (fixes the live B1 destructive write). Not started until Phase 0 is merged + green on staging (phase-per-PR). PR-1a (approval_batch family: seo_edit/audit_issue/schema_item/content_plan_*) DARK machinery landed: 5 self-registering adapters (audit_issue carries the real per-check field map + applyable=false for non-meta checks — B1 fix), send-time dual-write mirror gated on unified-deliverables-approval-family (default off → no-op), deterministic sub-type classifier + idempotent backfill script (scripts/backfill-deliverables-approval.ts, not auto-run). Apply stays disabled (D-apply). No reader/UI changes, no flag flip. Still pending: dual-write on response/apply paths, content_plan matrix-cell guard (CP-K4), nudge cron, backfill→shadow-compare→flag-flip. [UPDATE 2026-06-02: dual-write + backfill scripts built + flag-gated for all 5 physical families (PR-1a..1fg, #1013-#1018). REMAINING = shadow-compare (not yet built) -> owner-gated flag-flip -> backfill auto-run -> nudge cron. OWNER-GATED; the B1 destructive-write fix lands at the flip.]

items.title: Phase 2a — client unified inbox DARK behind unified-inbox flag (PriorityStrip + uniform Approve / Request changes / Decline)
items.notes: Strangler-fig client UI: additive flag-gated unified path in InboxTab (flag off → existing new-inbox-ia + legacy layouts byte-for-byte unchanged). New GET /api/public/deliverables/:workspaceId read endpoint assembles physical client_deliverable rows + projected copy/content_request (via adapter projectFromSource), filtered to client-facing statuses, Zod-validated response. NormalizedDecision generalized (add kind + widen DecisionSource to include 'deliverable', isSingleAction kept for back-compat) + normalizeDeliverable() adapter. useUnifiedInbox React Query hook (client- prefixed key, typed api wrapper) fetches only when flag on. When on, InboxTab renders PriorityStrip (mounted — was orphaned) as the single Needs-your-attention list with uniform Approve / Request changes (+note) / Decline calling the real PATCH /respond endpoint (dark). No flag flip; dual-write flags untouched. [DONE 2026-06-02 (PR #1019 + R2-R5 #1025-#1029 + finalize #1031): DARK unified client inbox — read endpoint, useUnifiedInbox flag-gated, PriorityStrip, uniform verbs, inline review, single-item modals. Cutover (flag-flip) tracked separately in phase-1-cutover. Verified by roadmap audit.]

items.title: Phase 2b — admin unified "Client Deliverables" pane DARK behind unified-inbox flag (status axis + stale + Remind)
items.notes: Strangler-fig admin UI (additive — AdminInbox/ClientActionsTab untouched on the flag-off path). New admin read endpoint GET /api/deliverables/:workspaceId (requireWorkspaceAccess, NOT requireAuth) returns EVERY status (physical + projected) via new listAllWorkspaceDeliverables() (the client-facing filter factored out into a shared assembleAllDeliverables), annotated by server/domains/inbox/admin-inbox-read.ts with statusAxis (awaiting_client/changes_requested[+partial]/approved/other), ageDays from sentAt, and a derived stale flag (awaiting_client + ageDays>=STALE_AWAITING_DAYS=7). Zod-validated response (shared/types/admin-deliverable-view.ts). New ClientDeliverablesPane.tsx groups by axis (oldest-first), shows pending-N-days/stale styling + type badge + a Remind button per awaiting_client item wired to the existing POST /api/deliverables/:ws/:id/remind. useWorkspaceDeliverables hook (admin- key, typed adminDeliverables api wrapper, no raw fetch, flag-gated fetch) + useWorkspaceEvents DELIVERABLE_* invalidation. Mounted dark as the first sub-tab in App.tsx's requests TabBar only when unified-inbox is ON (single additive branch: spread an empty tab array + skip the render when off). No flag flip; dual-write flags untouched. DEFERRED to cutover runbook: operator revise/resend/acknowledge on changes_requested (B18/E7 — needs new mutate endpoints), notification-bell categorization (E3/A2), workspace-overview B29 double-count, the read-path slice cutover.

items.title: R2 — respond propagation: unified respondToDeliverable writes back to the SOURCE artifact (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Fixed the linchpin silent no-op: respondToDeliverable previously wrote ONLY client_deliverable.status, so a unified-inbox client Approve never touched the real source artifact the operator/apply logic reads. Added an optional respondToSource(workspaceId, deliverable, decision, opts) to the adapter interface; every decision-bearing PHYSICAL adapter implements it (approval_batch family seo_edit/audit_issue/schema_item/content_plan_* → legacy approval batch; client_action family redirect/internal_link/aeo_change/content_decay → legacy client_action; schema_plan → schema_site_plans). Each maps the deliverable back to its source id via payload (legacyBatchId / legacyActionId / externalRef=siteId) and reuses the EXISTING source-write logic — extracted two route-inline bodies into shared services: server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-respond.ts (respondToApprovalBatch — whole-batch approve→items approved / changes_requested|declined→items rejected, the team email + APPROVAL_UPDATE broadcast + activity) now called by BOTH the public PATCH /api/public/approvals/:ws/:batch/approve route AND the unified path; server/domains/inbox/schema-plan-respond.ts (respondToSchemaPlanFeedback — approve→client_approved / request_changes→client_changes_requested + activity + SCHEMA_PLAN_SENT broadcast) now called by BOTH the public schema-plan /feedback route AND the unified path; client_action reuses the existing respondToPublicClientAction (lazy import to break the adapter↔mutations module cycle). respondToDeliverable now calls adapter.respondToSource after writing the mirror and SUPPRESSES its own deliverable-level team email when the source path reports handled (no double-notify); the DELIVERABLE_UPDATED broadcast is always kept for the unified inbox UI. R2 propagates only the DECISION/status — apply (Webflow publish) stays a separate step (R3, D-apply unchanged). work_order/briefing (no client decision, R5) and projected copy/content_request (R4) get no respondToSource; respond on a projected/unknown id still 404s. Tests: tests/integration/respond-propagation.test.ts seeds BOTH a client_deliverable mirror AND its source artifact per family and asserts (a) the SOURCE status changed, (b) the mirror status changed, (c) team-notify fired exactly once; contract guard extended in tests/contract/inbox-route-service-extraction.test.ts. No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox (the unified respond is only reachable when the inbox is on).

items.title: R3a — deliverable detail + per-item review: surface the substance in the unified inbox + flag/subset-approve (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Fixed the owner complaint that the unified inbox sends the task, not the content. The card's 'View N →' now opens a new full-screen DeliverableDetailModal (src/components/client/DeliverableDetailModal.tsx) rendering the SUBSTANCE from the deliverable's carried NormalizedDecision.items + .payload (no second fetch). REUSED the proven legacy renderers by EXTRACTING them into src/components/client/decision-renderers.tsx (ApprovalItemRow, AeoRenderer, InternalLinkRenderer, RedirectRenderer) plus a new presentational ItemDiffRow that both the legacy DecisionDetailModal and the new modal drive (DRY); legacy modal behavior unchanged. Approval family (seo_edit/audit_issue/schema_item/content_plan_*) → typed items[] rows with PER-ITEM Flag/Unflag + 'Looks good — implement N of M →' subset CTA; client_action family (redirect/internal_link/aeo_change) → payload.items fed read-only to the matching renderer wrapper with whole-action Approve/Request changes/Decline (no per-item flag — matches legacy). One respond path: modal approve forwards flagged ClientDeliverableItem.id`s through the EXISTING useRespondToDeliverable → PATCH /respond (new optional flaggedItemIds: z.array(z.string()).max(500).optional()), extending R2. Server: respondToApprovalBatch gained an optional per-item mode (itemDecisions: {legacyItemId,status,note}[]) — when present, drives updateItem per legacy item (unflagged→approved, flagged→rejected) inside the existing db.transaction(); absent → whole-batch approve-all-pending (back-compat). respondToApprovalBatchSource builds itemDecisions from deliverable.items (item→legacy approval_item.id via itemPayload.legacyItemId, skip+warn on no mapping) only on approved with non-empty flags; the mirror status stays approved (implement N of M); changes_requested/declined ignore flags. Per-item flag notes thread onto each held item's clientNote. content_decay stays inline single-action; projected types keep their read-only deep-link. Confirmed getDeliverable() DOES load items so responded.items carries itemPayload.legacyItemId. NOT the Apply-to-Website PR (that is R3b — Webflow apply unchanged/disabled, D-apply). Tests: tests/integration/respond-per-item-propagation.test.ts (port-free service-level; subset approve, multi-flag, back-compat approve-all, empty-array back-compat, reject-ignores-flags, client_action-ignores-flags) + tests/unit/DeliverableDetailModal.test.tsx (deliverable→renderer mapping per family + per-item flag CTA + onApprove carries flagged id). No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox.

items.title: R3b — Apply to Website: explicit post-approve publish step in the unified inbox, reusing the EXISTING Webflow apply route (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner requirement: a SEPARATE, explicit 'Apply to Website' step after approve that goes through the proven Webflow apply route (ZERO new apply logic). The client calls the SAME POST /api/public/approvals/:ws/:batch/apply route the legacy ApprovalBatchCard uses (it already does updatePageSeo/updateCollectionItem/publishCollectionItems, the server-side applyability gate, markBatchApplied, activity, outcome tracking, broadcasts). Added ONLY: (1) shared/applyability.ts — the SINGLE source of truth applyability predicate (field/targetRef/collectionId), intentionally mirroring the LEGACY ROUTE GATE, NOT the per-item applyable column (which is hardcoded false for the whole approval family under D-apply); the legacy src/components/client/approvalApplyability.ts now delegates to it (behavior-preserving), and the server route gate (server/routes/approvals.ts) + its local isCmsSeoApprovalField were replaced with isClientApplyableFields (the two in-loop cms- guards kept as defense-in-depth). (2) Server mirror-flip markDeliverableApplied (server/domains/inbox/send-to-client.ts) — body of applyApprovedDeliverable MINUS the external apply() call (Webflow write already done by the legacy route), idempotent on already-applied; called from the /apply route AFTER markBatchApplied, gated on APPROVAL_FAMILY_FLAG (NOT unified-inbox), wrapped in try/catch that logs+swallows (a mirror miss must not fail the apply response). isFeatureEnabled is single-arg/global. The disabled applyDisabledStub stays wired (appliesOnApprove omitted) — D-apply holds; the mirror-flip does NOT call adapter.applyDeliverable. (3) Client: publicDeliverables.applyApproval wrapper + useApplyDeliverable hook (invalidates the unified inbox key); a new 'Ready to publish' section in UnifiedInbox.tsx (approved + client-applyable deliverables, since approved is NOT in ACTIONABLE_STATUSES but IS client-facing) rendering an 'Apply to Website' primary button (ConfirmDialog 'Apply to live site?') + 'View N changes →'; DeliverableDetailModal gained canApply/applying/onApply props rendering a single full-width Apply button instead of the approve/decline row. Post-apply the deliverable flips to applied (filtered OUT of CLIENT_FACING_STATUSES) so the item LEAVES the inbox + the modal auto-unmounts (intended UX; success toast confirms). Held/subset-rejected items are never applied (the route's status==='approved' filter excludes them). schema_item/schema_plan/content_plan_*/client_action get NO button (the shared predicate excludes them; the route gate rejects them). Tests: tests/integration/r3b-apply-to-website.test.ts (5: applyable seo_edit flips mirror, subset-held not applied + mirror still flips, schema_item 400 no flip, flag-off byte-identical no flip, markDeliverableApplied idempotency) + tests/unit/applyability-shared.test.ts (incl. the documented divergence: applyable:false items still canApply=true) + DeliverableDetailModal Apply-footer cases. No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox + APPROVAL_FAMILY_FLAG.

items.title: R4 — in-shell projected review: mount the bespoke copy/content review surfaces inside the unified inbox so the client reviews + responds without leaving (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner requirement: the two PROJECTED deliverable types (copy_section / content_request) had no physical row; R3a rendered them as a read-only 'Review →' deep-link that navigated the client OUT to ?tab=reviews. R4 mounts the PROVEN bespoke review surfaces IN-SHELL so the client reviews + responds 'once and for all' without leaving. Added ONLY: (1) src/components/client/inbox/ProjectedReviewModal.tsx — a thin full-screen modal wrapper (chrome + Escape-to-close guard copied verbatim from DeliverableDetailModal, incl. the isContentEditable/input/textarea/select guard so the RichTextEditor + useAutoSave inside PostReviewCard never lose typing on a stray Escape; WIDE max-w-5xl panel since ContentTab renders a PageHeader + 4-col stat grid + pipeline list). Branches on TYPE (never deliverable status): copy_section → <ClientCopyReview initialExpandedEntryId={externalRef}/>; content_request → <ContentTab {...passThrough} initialExpandedRequestId={externalRef}/> (ContentTab self-selects brief actions vs PostReviewCard from the LOCAL ClientContentRequest.status — both client_review brief + post_review post map to canonical awaiting_client, the raw status lives only in payload.contentRequestStatus). (2) Additive OPTIONAL props on the shared bespoke components (legacy-safe, default = current behavior): ClientCopyReview.initialExpandedEntryId (threaded outer wrapper → inner → seeds expandedEntryId useState); ContentTab.initialExpandedRequestId → useContentRequests seeds expandedContentReq. PostReviewCard unchanged (reached transitively); DecisionCard.onReview contract unchanged (only the callback body swapped in UnifiedInbox). (3) UnifiedInbox: reviewProjected state; projected onReview now opens the modal (reading raw d.type + d.externalRef — normalizeDeliverable drops both) instead of goToReviews (deleted, with its dead navigate/betaMode locals + useNavigate/useBetaMode/clientPath imports — tsc noUnusedLocals confirms zero unused); modal mounted near DeliverableDetailModal; UnifiedInboxProps extended with the ContentTab pass-through props (Omit<ContentTabProps,'workspaceId'|'setToast'|'initialExpandedRequestId'>), threaded from ClientDashboard → InboxTab (NOT hasCopyEntries — it's a ClientCopyReview gate, not a ContentTab prop). (4) MANDATORY unified-list refresh: the bespoke respond routes broadcast COPY_SECTION_UPDATED / CONTENT_REQUEST_UPDATE / POST_UPDATED, which UnifiedInbox did NOT listen on — added 3 additive handlers (flag-ON-only) each invalidating queryKeys.client.unifiedInbox so after an in-shell respond the card leaves the list + the modal can be dismissed; all 3 events already broadcast + handled elsewhere so broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts stays green. Respond NEVER hits the unified /respond (projected ids 404 there — getDeliverable returns null → SendToClientError 404). BetaProvider confirmed already above the modal (ClientDashboard wraps the dashboard at :637). Tests: updated tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx (the 2 projected tests now assert 'Review →' OPENS the in-shell modal + does NOT navigate, with real externalRefs); new tests/component/ProjectedReviewModal.test.tsx (copy→ClientCopyReview auto-expanded, content→ContentTab auto-expanded + pass-through, Escape guarded in editable / closes elsewhere, backdrop close); new tests/integration/projected-review-respond-routes.test.ts (port 13877: content-request approve emits CONTENT_REQUEST_UPDATE, copy approve emits COPY_SECTION_UPDATED, + NEGATIVE projected content_request:/copy: ids 404 on unified /respond). No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox (flag-off + legacy ?tab=reviews surfaces byte-identical).

items.title: R5 — work_order TRACK lane: read-only order-progress lane in the unified inbox (NO approve/decline verbs, DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner decision: work orders must LIVE in the unified inbox but as a read/TRACK lane — STATUS the client FOLLOWS (work the agency does FOR them), NOT a decision they make → ZERO approve/decline/apply/review verbs. (briefing stays OUT — it's surfaced on the dashboard homepage, not the inbox.) CRITICAL pre-fix: work_order rows were NOT client-facing today — work_order statuses are ordered|in_progress|completed|cancelled, NONE of which were in CLIENT_FACING_STATUSES, so they were filtered OUT of the client list. R5 needed BOTH a server read change AND a client render path. SERVER (server/domains/inbox/unified-inbox-read.ts): added CLIENT_FACING_ORDER_STATUSES={ordered,in_progress,completed} (cancelled excluded) + a KIND-AWARE predicate isClientFacingDeliverable(d) that admits the order-lifecycle statuses ONLY for kind:'order' and uses isClientFacingDeliverableStatus for every other kind; listClientFacingDeliverables now filters on it. isClientFacingDeliverableStatus stays exported+unchanged (other callers); listAllWorkspaceDeliverables (admin pane) untouched (already returns all statuses). Gated on KIND, not type, so no review/decision/batch row can leak ordered/in_progress/completed into the client list. The work_order adapter + dual-write needed NO change (they already produce correct rows — they were just filtered out). CLIENT (src/components/client/inbox/UnifiedInbox.tsx): added module-level ORDER_STATUS_CHIP (Four Laws — ordered=amber, in_progress=blue+Loader2 spin, completed=emerald; NEVER teal for the chip) + OrderTrackStepper over the canonical steps [ordered,in_progress,completed] (completed-step dots/connectors use teal to mark completed PROGRESS, a data affordance, consistent with the legacy OrderStatus stepper) + a workOrders useMemo (deliverables.filter kind==='order'). New dedicated read-only <section> 'Work in progress' (mirrors the R3b 'Ready to publish' container: surface-2 + radius-signature-lg w/ the pr-check-disable-next-line brand-radius hatch). Each card: status chip, title, summary, OrderTrackStepper (SKIPPED for completed), age (ageLabel), and a COUNT-ONLY page summary ('N pages') — raw payload.pageIds are raw Webflow ids and are NEVER surfaced (CLAUDE.md 'never surface raw IDs'). The card is PLAIN markup: NO normalizeDeliverable, NO DecisionCard, NO onApprove/onFlagWithNote/onDecline/onReview/onApply wired (structural verb-safety — there is no verb to wire). Orders never enter `actionable` (ACTIONABLE_STATUSES has no order status) so they never reach the PriorityStrip; PriorityStrip's sectionForKind case 'order' left as-is (unreachable). Orders-only empty-state FIX: the 'Nothing needs your attention' guard is now actionable.length===0 && workOrders.length===0 so it does NOT show when there are orders to track. NOTE (intentional, out-of-scope): the legacy src/components/client/OrderStatus.tsx + its route /api/public/work-orders/:ws stay LIVE — R5 introduces a SECOND client-facing surface for work-order data (the unified track lane) and intentionally does NOT retire the legacy component/route; reconciliation is a future follow-up. No new flag (DARK behind unified-inbox fetch flag + unified-deliverables-rest mirror flag, both default false). Tests: tests/integration/work-order-track-lane-read.test.ts (port 13878: kind:'order' in ordered/in_progress/completed INCLUDED, cancelled EXCLUDED, CROSS-LEAK guard seo_edit/batch in in_progress/ordered EXCLUDED, review-status rows still included, client-portal auth still 401s) + extended tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx (track section renders title/summary/chip/stepper, ZERO verbs + mutations never called, order absent from PriorityStrip, completed renders chip but no stepper, orders-only empty-state suppressed, flag OFF no section).

items.title: Work-order conversation (client↔team comments) + explicit `closed` state + admin conversation/close panel (PRODUCTION data model/routes/admin; client thread DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner decisions: focused admin conversation/close panel (NOT a full dashboard tab); email both ways; dedicated comments table; operator-only close; completed→closed one-way (NO reopen); comments OUT-OF-BAND from the deliverable payload. DARK SPLIT — PRODUCTION (not flag-gated): the data model, state machine, migration, store, routes, notifications, the mirror exhaustive-switch arm, and the ADMIN conversation/close panel. DARK (behind unified-inbox): only the CLIENT-facing conversation thread + comment input inside the 'Work in progress' track-lane card. STATE MACHINE (server/state-machines.ts): WORK_ORDER_TRANSITIONS completed→['closed'] (was []), closed:[] terminal; added 'closed' to WorkOrderStatus. No completed→in_progress reopen. SHARED (shared/types/payments.ts): 'closed' added to WorkOrder.status; optional closedAt?; new WorkOrderComment{id,workOrderId,author:'client'|'team',content,createdAt,readAt?} + WorkOrderCommentAuthor. MIGRATION 113-work-order-comments.sql: CREATE TABLE work_order_comments (FK→work_orders + workspaces, both ON DELETE CASCADE) + 2 indexes + ALTER TABLE work_orders ADD COLUMN closed_at (the runner detects ADD COLUMN → per-statement exec w/ duplicate-column tolerance, so mixed CREATE+ALTER is safe; append-only). STORE: closed_at lockstep in server/work-orders.ts (OrderRow/insert/update/rowToOrder/createWorkOrder writes null/updateWorkOrder accepts closedAt). New server/work-order-comments.ts (sibling module): listWorkOrderComments + addWorkOrderComment (null if parent missing; the read+insert+parent-updated_at bump wrapped in db.transaction(...).immediate() — BEGIN IMMEDIATE avoids the SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT read-then-write race); own stmt-cache + rowToComment (three-state read_at). Comments are NOT a field on WorkOrder/OrderRow (served out-of-band). ROUTES: admin PATCH (server/routes/work-orders.ts) added 'closed' to the Zod enum + a closed branch (set closedAt + order_closed activity; does NOT re-enter the completed page-state/email block); admin comment POST (author 'team') + GET; client public comment POST (server/routes/public-content.ts, behind the router-level requireClientPortalAuth prefix gate, author HARDCODED 'client', 409 when closed) + public GET. New server/schemas/work-orders.ts workOrderCommentSchema {content: string.min(1).max(2000)}.strict(). EVENTS: WS_EVENTS.WORK_ORDER_COMMENT='work-order:comment' (server + frontend mirror). ACTIVITY: 'work_order_commented' + 'order_closed' added to ActivityType + CLIENT_VISIBLE_TYPES. EMAIL: notifyTeamWorkOrderComment (client→team, internal) + notifyClientWorkOrderComment (team→client, action) + 2 templates + email-throttle category map entries; best-effort, never blocks the write. MIRROR (server/domains/inbox/deliverable-adapters/work-order.ts): 'closed' case → 'cancelled' (leaves the client lane, ∉ CLIENT_FACING_ORDER_STATUSES; raw status preserved in payload.workOrderStatus) — satisfies the exhaustive never-guard; completed stays 'completed' (stays in lane). ADMIN PANEL: src/components/admin/WorkOrderPanel.tsx — focused full-screen modal (mirrors DeliverableDetailModal chrome) opened from WorkspaceHome's 'purchased fixes awaiting fulfillment' attention item (the item now carries onClick→setWorkOrderPanelOpen instead of navigating to workspace-settings). Lists orders → select → order detail + client↔team conversation + reply box (author 'team') + Start work / Mark complete / Close out (ConfirmDialog, destructive). No purple (not AI). New src/api/work-orders.ts (workOrders + publicWorkOrders typed wrappers) + src/hooks/admin/useWorkOrders.ts (list/comments/status PATCH/comment mutation). queryKeys.admin.workOrders + workOrderComments. CLIENT LANE (DARK): the 'Work in progress' card's map body extracted into a module-level WorkOrderTrackCard sub-component (so a hook can run per card) that renders the existing verb-free card PLUS the client↔team conversation thread (public GET via useClientWorkOrderComments) + a comment input (public POST via usePostClientWorkOrderComment, author 'client'). Order id extracted from sourceRef ('work_order:<id>'). Input gated on workOrderStatus !== 'closed' (closed leaves the lane anyway). Lane stays verb-free — NO approve/decline/apply; the conversation input is the ONLY interactive element. queryKeys.client.workOrderComments. WS HANDLERS: WORK_ORDER_COMMENT handled on BOTH the admin side (useWsInvalidation → admin workOrderComments+workOrders) and the client side (UnifiedInbox wsHandlers → client workOrderComments + unified inbox) so broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts stays green. TESTS: state-machine (completed→closed legal, closed terminal, illegal in_progress→closed/closed→* rejected; existing assertions updated); store unit (work-order-comments.test.ts: insert/null-missing-parent/ordered/scope/closedAt); routes integration (work-order-conversation-close.test.ts port 13409: admin PATCH→closed sets closedAt+activity, admin comment POST author team + GET, client public comment forces 'client'+409-when-closed+401-unauth-on-pw-workspace+.strict()-rejects-author, illegal close 400); mirror arm in work-order-adapter.test.ts (closed→cancelled); client lane component (InboxTabUnified.test.tsx: conversation thread+input render for non-closed w/ sourceRef, post calls mutation, input hidden when closed, still no decision verbs). NOTE: the deferred unified-inbox-deferred-ia-polish item (a) WORK_ORDER realtime gap is partially addressed — the WORK_ORDER_COMMENT handler invalidates the unified inbox; the WORK_ORDER_UPDATE→unified-inbox invalidation + completed-order lane decay remain in that item.

items.title: Deferred IA polish for the unified inbox (completed-order lane decay + WORK_ORDER realtime gap, ?tab= deep-link under the flag, PriorityStrip↔section-heading taxonomy alignment)
items.notes: Deferred from the unified-inbox finalize-UX PR (feat/unified-inbox-finalize-ux). Three IA-polish items captured for a follow-up, NOT built in that PR: (a) COMPLETED work orders should LEAVE the 'Work in progress' lane (recency window or collapse) so the lane only shows live work — AND close the WORK_ORDER_UPDATE realtime-refresh gap: the work-order mirror create/update does not currently invalidate the unified inbox query (no WS event handler), so a status change isn't reflected live (CLAUDE.md Data Flow #2 two-halves contract). The completed-order lane-decay concern is SUPERSEDED by the work-order CLOSED state (#1032): completed now intentionally STAYS (conversation-active) and closed LEAVES the lane. REMAINING = ?tab= deep-link resolution under the unified-inbox flag (UnifiedInbox ignores searchParams) + PriorityStrip taxonomy/section headings.]

sprints.name: 🧠 Recommendation Intelligence — Integrity Remediation + Opportunity Value Re-Architecture

The build is complete, the runtime cutover is now canonical, and this sprint tracks the remaining hardening/history items.

items.notes: PRs #997-#999 + a direct staging push (4 phases). Phase 1: applied SEO changes/approvals/work-orders/publishes resolve+regenerate the rec set (resolveRecommendationsForChange). Phase 2 (#997): migration 106 action_outcomes.attributed_value (clicks-delta x CPC); digest/learnings re-pointed off the dead roi_attributions. Phase 3 (#998): effectiveBusinessPriorities resolver + ranking + summary.topRecommendationId. Phase 4 (#999): intelligence-cache invalidation across write sites, new slice fields, admin-chat formatter routing, SEO-write rec-resolution. FEATURE_AUDIT #467.

items.title: Unified Opportunity Value scorer — 7-phase build
items.notes: PRs #1001-#1007. computeOpportunityValue (server/scoring/) + CTR-curve; opportunity persistence (Zod lockstep); Spine-A wiring via the legacy↔OV selector chokepoint; shadow divergence logging (ov_divergence, migration 107); referring-domains authority + realized-outcome calibration + weights (migrations 108/109); advisor+client coherence (client sees ROI badge, never $); opportunity_events ledger + decaying Timing + decay/competitor/rank-drop detectors (migration 110). items.title: P8 — owner-gated Opportunity Value cutover (shadow review then flip)

The temporary rollout flags (`opportunity-value-scorer`, `opportunity-value-calibration`, `opportunity-value-events`) were retired, Opportunity Value became the canonical impactScore/runtime path, and event-driven timing became default-on. items.title: SEO Generation Quality P0 — quality harness + per-workspace flag + guardrails
items.notes: Phase 0 of the SEO strategy + keyword generation quality plan. Pure additive infrastructure, no generation-output behavior change. Ships: (1) per-workspace feature-flag dimension (isFeatureEnabled(flag, workspaceId?), migration 114, setWorkspaceFlagOverride) — also satisfies ov-per-workspace-flag-dimension; (2) the 'seo-generation-quality' umbrella flag + 'SEO Generation Quality' group + lifecycle catalog entry; (3) a typed GenerationQuality telemetry interface (shared/types/generation-quality.ts) emitted from keyword-strategy-generation.ts (poolSize + aiReturnedCount knowable now; suppressedCount/backfilledCount/floorHit populated by P1-P2); (4) two advisory/soft eval fixtures (sparse-workspace contentGaps>=6, malformed-AI-response throws) — RED until P1-P3, never hard-fail; (5) docs/rules/seo-generation-quality.md guardrail doc + two forward-looking pr-check rules (admin-money field must be stripped; new RecType/RecSource needs category + action-type). items.title: SEO Generation Quality P1 — buildKeywordUniverse (full pool + geo + language + MCP seed)
items.notes: Phase 1 of the SEO generation-quality plan. Ships DARK behind isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id) — flag-OFF is byte-identical (the legacy inline pool build + seoDataMode==='full' discovery fetch are kept verbatim in the else branch; a flag-OFF parity test asserts the universe-built pool matches the legacy build). Ships: (1) typed contract shared/types/keyword-universe.ts (KEYWORD_CANDIDATE_SOURCE const enum + KeywordCandidate + KeywordUniverse); (2) new server/keyword-strategy-universe.ts buildKeywordUniverse(workspaceId, opts) folding the ENTIRE inline pool build (domain/GSC/competitor/gaps/discovery/related/client-tracked/client-requested + branded + declined hard-filters) AND owning the geo+language-threaded provider discovery fetch, keeping the canonical Map and reusing the shared admission funnel; (3) resolveWorkspaceLanguageCode in local-seo.ts (primary-market latest-snapshot language, 'en' fallback) + existing resolveWorkspaceLocationCode threaded into every pool-path provider call; (4) language threading at all 7 DataForSEO pool-path sites (getKeywordMetrics x2, getRelatedKeywords, getQuestionKeywords, getKeywordSuggestions, getKeywordIdeas, getKeywordsForSite) replacing the language_code:'en' literals; (5) versioned + language-aware metrics-cache key (v2:<locationCode>:<lang>) so a non-US/non-en flag-ON workspace cannot read/write rows other-geo/flag-OFF workspaces consume; (6) MCP-seed fix — flag-ON promotes the collapsed seoDataMode 'none' to 'quick' when a provider exists so MCP/chat generations build a real universe; (7) per-workspace monthly provider-call ceiling enforced IN the assembler (not the shared creditExhaustedUntil breaker); (8) telemetry suppressedCount wired from the assembler. No DB migration (cache key versioned via token prefix). P2 attaches the deterministic backfill floor; P3 attaches per-candidate declined/requested/voteWeight/priority annotations to KeywordCandidate (already optional fields). Unblocks P2. P1 ADVERSARIAL-REVIEW FIXES (2026-06-02): (C1) threaded geo (locationCode) into the 5 discovery calls — the original commit threaded only language and passed undefined in the database slot, so non-US flag-ON workspaces still got US-located discovery (locationCodeFromDatabase('us')=2840); added optional locationCode?:number to the 5 discovery methods on the SeoDataProvider interface + DataForSEO impl (locationCode ?? locationCodeFromDatabase(database)) and the assembler now passes the resolved locationCode; SemRush left untouched (language/geo-blind for discovery). (C2) removed the seenDiscovery/seenRelated short-circuit that dropped an eligible-later duplicate after an ineligible/lower-vol first row — dedup now relies on upsertKeywordPoolCandidate's Map + higher-volume tiebreak exactly like the legacy fold. (I2) gated the legacy seoDataMode==='full' discovery/related/question prefetch in keyword-strategy-seo-data.ts on !isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id) so flag-ON no longer double-fetches; the prompt gets discovery context via the universe KEYWORD POOL block. (I4) flag-ON per-page metrics validation now passes resolveWorkspaceLanguageCode(ws.id). (M1) restored exact pre-filter clientKeywordsAdded counting on flag-OFF (byte-identical prompt) and collapsed the let/const shadow. (M2) wrapped buildKeywordUniverse so a throw degrades to the legacy fold instead of aborting generation. (M3) doc note that SemRush is language/geo-blind for discovery. (I3) flag-OFF parity test now drives the REAL else-branch via the exported buildLegacyKeywordPool, and the ceiling test uses FakeSeoProvider. flag-OFF remains byte-identical.

items.title: SEO Generation Quality P2 — relax conservatism + deterministic backfill floor + backfilled honesty
items.notes: Phase 2 of the SEO generation-quality plan. All behavior changes ship behind isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id); the flag is computed ONCE per generation and threaded as a relaxConservatism boolean into the eligibility/prune/backfill logic (no isFeatureEnabled in hot loops). Flag-OFF is byte-identical to today (verified by a flag-OFF parity test). Ships: (a) business_mismatch hard-suppress DEMOTION (rules.ts isStrategyPoolEligibleKeyword keeps the -18 penalty but drops suppressed=true on flag-ON so narrow-but-real keywords survive into ranking; relaxConservatism on KeywordEvaluationContext, threaded via buildStrategyKeywordEvaluationContext); (b) token-subset page-coverage prune (_removePageCoveredContentGaps / isTopicKeywordCoveredByPageMap switch substring .includes() to token-subset containment on flag-ON so 'dental implants cost' is not eaten by a /dental-implants page); (c) KD-0 long-tail (isStrategyQualityDiscoveryKeyword takes relaxConservatism: flag-ON allows difficulty >= 0 behind STRATEGY_QUALITY_KD_ZERO_VOLUME_FLOOR (>=10); threaded only into the flag-ON assembler, legacy buildLegacyKeywordPool unchanged); (d) deterministic backfill floor (backfillContentGapsToFloor, STRATEGY_CONTENT_GAP_FLOOR=6 owner-approved) re-admits the highest-scoring PRUNED candidates (enrichKeywordStrategy.prunedContentGaps) by score until the floor is met, tagging backfilled=true; deterministic (no AI), admits-what's-available if <6 exist; wired into generation after the post-enrichment sanitizer, before persist; (e) FULL backfilled DB lockstep in one commit: migration 115 (ALTER content_gaps ADD backfilled INTEGER DEFAULT 0) -> ContentGapRow -> rowToModel()+modelToParams() -> INSERT/ON CONFLICT (server/content-gaps.ts) -> public field whitelist (routes/public-content.ts seo-strategy) -> client ContentGap type (src/components/client/types.ts) -> renderer sort + 'Expanded pick' zinc badge (StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection.tsx, backfilled gaps sort after organically-strong ones, no purple); contentGaps are NOT in keywordStrategySchema (deleted from the blob + normalized at #365) so no Schema-vs-stored-shape change; (f) recommendations_ready email de-pad (routes/jobs.ts subtracts the backfilled-gap count from recs.length so the headline count is honest); (g) telemetry GenerationQuality.suppressedCount (from synthesis) + backfilledCount + floorHit (from the backfill) populated from the real run (0/false on flag-OFF); (h) eval-fixture promotion: the P0 advisory it.todo 'sparse workspace produces contentGaps >= 6' is now a HARD assertion + the ai-reliability-registry soft fixture seo-gen-quality-sparse-content-gaps flipped soft->hard; the malformed-AI it.todo stays advisory (P3). Tests: flag-OFF parity + flag-ON behavior unit tests, a PUBLIC read-path integration test (GET /api/public/seo-strategy/:id, backfilled gap renders through the whitelist), and a generation-path integration test (flag-ON sparse Faros fixture fills to 6 + tags; flag-OFF stays at 2). Unblocks P3 (the backfill is P3's never-emit-empty fallback).

items.title: SEO Generation Quality P3 — Zod-validated named ops + closed-set grounded prompting + client-signal contract
items.notes: Phase 3 of the SEO generation-quality plan. The closed-set path ships behind isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id) (seoGenQualityEnabled, computed once); flag-OFF is byte-identical (the legacy callKeywordStrategyAI/parseJsonFallback batch + master prompts and the master throw are kept VERBATIM in the else branches). Ships: (1) two named ops registered in server/ai-operation-registry.ts — 'keyword-page-assignment' (OP1) + 'keyword-site-synthesis' (OP2): outputMode json, defaultResponseFormat json_object, defaultModel gpt-5.4-mini, defaultResearchMode true (flag-ON-only prompt divergence documented in the contract), defaultMaxRetries 3, defaultTimeoutMs 90000, parserExpectation 'parseJsonSafe + zod, retry-once then deterministic backfill'. (2) NEW server/schemas/keyword-strategy-schemas.ts — pageAssignmentResponseSchema ({ assignments: [...] }, json_object requires a top-level object so OP1 returns an object not a bare array; item: pagePath/pageTitle/primaryKeyword/primaryKeywordSourceId/secondaryKeywords.default([])/searchIntent?/justification?) + siteSynthesisResponseSchema ({ siteKeywords, opportunities, contentGaps, quickWins, keywordFixes }, validating the AI-returned subset); field names cross-checked against PageMapping/PageKeywordMap + ContentGap/StrategyContentGap/QuickWin; uses .safeParse (NOT a throwing parser) capturing .error.issues for the retry. (3) callNamedStrategyAI(workspaceId, operation, messages, maxTokens) in keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts mirrors callKeywordStrategyAI but takes operation as a param (does NOT route through callKeywordStrategyAI which hardcodes operation:'keyword-strategy'); reuses buildSystemPrompt. (4) OP1 flag-ON branch in runBatch: closed-set prompt -> keyword-page-assignment -> safeParse(parseJsonFallback(raw)) -> on fail retry ONCE appending the raw + a user turn quoting issues.slice(0,5) -> map assignments->PageMapping[] (primaryKeywordSourceId in the pool short-circuits eligibility) and run the EXISTING post-processing VERBATIM (factored into postProcessBatch) -> on still-fail fall to the EXISTING per-page synthetic fallback (never empty). (5) OP2 flag-ON branch in master synthesis: closed-set prompt + business-priorities context -> keyword-site-synthesis -> safeParse -> retry-once -> on still-fail use a TYPED-EMPTY object (the master throw is removed ONLY on the flag-ON path) -> EXISTING post-AI filters run VERBATIM (branded/declined/eligibility) -> requested re-add (G4: any requested candidate not covered by pageMap AND absent from finalContentGaps is injected as a content gap, priority high — hard guarantee) -> synthesis-internal never-emit-empty backfill (build prunedFromUniverse from universe.candidates excluding declined + already-selected, call backfillContentGapsToFloor; COMPLEMENTARY to P2's generation-layer backfill, idempotent via the seen de-dup). (6) universe.candidates captured (synthesis previously discarded universe). (7) G4 client-signal contract: BuildKeywordUniverseOptions gains contentGapVotes + businessPriorities, threaded from the synthesis call site; the candidate-derivation loop in keyword-strategy-universe.ts now populates KeywordCandidate annotations declined/requested/voteWeight/priority (previously never set); the closed-set prompt enumerates candidates by stable source-row id (normalized keyword) + annotations and instructs SELECT + JUSTIFY by id; businessPriorities injected as global OP2 context. Tests: tests/integration/seo-genquality-p3-fm2-named-ops.test.ts (FM-2 flag-ON malformed -> retry fires -> backfill -> NON-EMPTY contentGaps; flag-OFF malformed -> legacy throw; client-signal flag-ON requested re-add + declined never appears) + the P0 malformed-AI eval it.todo promoted to a HARD never-empty assertion in tests/contract/seo-generation-quality-evals.test.ts and the ai-reliability-registry seo-gen-quality-malformed-ai-throws fixture flipped soft->hard. flag-OFF parity preserved (existing keyword-strategy suite passes unchanged). [P3 REVIEW 2026-06-03 — flag-ON-only, flag-OFF still byte-identical: (I1) CLOSED-SET MEMBERSHIP now ENFORCED — synthesis builds candidateIds = Set(universeCandidates.map(normalizeKeyword(keyword))) once and a resolveClosedSetKeyword(sourceId, keyword) helper accepts the AI's *SourceId only if it is a real candidate id, else the keyword only if THAT is in the set, else null. OP1: an out-of-set pick passes the raw keyword through to postProcessBatch's eligibility+per-page-fallback (rejects the hallucination, never lets a hallucinated id override a valid in-set keyword). OP2: an out-of-set targetKeyword/sourceId DROPS the gap (never-emit-empty backfill re-fills). New test: out-of-set sourceId/keyword is not admitted and does not override. (M1) OP1 post-retry fallback changed from syntheticBatchFallback (empty/_parseError rows -> filtered out -> zero surviving pages, the old comment lied) to a real per-page perPageFallbackBatch() via findFallbackKeywordForPage(p.path) so pages survive a parse-fail; flag-OFF keeps the verbatim synthetic fallback; misleading comment corrected. (M2) requested-re-add HARD guarantee now survives enrichment — re-added requested gaps carry a TRANSIENT requested:true marker (StrategyContentGap; NOT persisted — content_gaps write path uses an explicit field list) and _removePageCoveredContentGaps SKIPS marked gaps so the covered-topic prune can't silently undo it (FIX path chosen — clean, no disproportionate threading; documented in docs/rules/seo-generation-quality.md). (M3) dead businessPriorities option dropped from BuildKeywordUniverseOptions + the call site (it was void-ed/unused; OP2 sources priorities from clientSignals.effectiveBusinessPriorities directly); contentGapVotes kept (used for voteWeight annotations).]

items.title: SEO Generation Quality P4 — predictedEmv survival + OV-derived tier + one gain basis + brief-cache fix
items.notes: Phase 4 of the SEO generation-quality plan. FLAG LAYERING: predictedEmv compute/persist/schema/strip + the ovClone re-tier in the divergence shadow log are ALWAYS-ON (dark/admin-only); the OV-derived served TIER, the OV-EMV estimatedGain basis, and the content_gaps.opportunity_score recompute are gated on isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id), resolved ONCE per rec-gen cycle as a useGenQual boolean next to useOv (NOT the global opportunity-value-scorer flag). Umbrella-OFF is byte-identical to pre-P4 (proven by a flag-OFF snapshot test). Ships: (PART C, one commit, 6-leg lockstep) OpportunityScore.predictedEmv (CPC-proxy placeholder JSDoc) -> computeOpportunityValue returns Math.round(emvPerWeek*HORIZON_WEEKS) (always-on) -> opportunityScoreSchema.predictedEmv: z.number() (closed schema, survives reload) -> stripEmvFromPublicRecs strips predictedEmv (+ sanitizes estimatedGain) -> migration 116 tracked_actions.predicted_emv + tracked_actions_archive.predicted_emv + TrackedActionRow/rowToTrackedAction + TrackedAction.predictedEmv? + recordAction insert col-list/VALUES/payload + RecordActionParams.predictedEmv? + write site 1 (routes/recommendations.ts rec.opportunity?.predictedEmv ?? null) + write site 2 (outcome-backfill.ts null, documented) + getCalibrationOutcomes SELECT ta.predicted_emv + CalibrationOutcome.predictedEmv -> ov-calibration.ts swap-site doc updated (P4 carries the field, does NOT change the basis; P6 does). ARCHIVE BUG FIXED: archiveOld was a positional INSERT ... SELECT *, datetime('now') — ALTER appends predicted_emv after updated_at on the source but after archived_at on the archive, so SELECT * mapped predicted_emv->archived_at and datetime('now')->predicted_emv (corruption). Converted archiveOld to an EXPLICIT column list (order-independent), the same fix migration 106 applied to archiveOldOutcomes; pinned by the archive round-trip test. (PART D, useGenQual) deriveOvTier(rec) chokepoint AFTER pickImpactScore + BEFORE sortRecommendations overwrites rec.priority from OV value bands (OWNER-TUNABLE thresholds: value>=70 fix_now, >=45 fix_soon, >=20 fix_later, else ongoing) EXCEPT genuine CRITICAL_CHECKS (kept fix_now); flag-OFF no-op so sortRecommendations/priorityOrder/computeRecommendationSummary are byte-identical. ovClone tier fix (always-on, G1): recordOvDivergence takes deriveTier (injected, no cycle), the OV clone re-tiers before sorting; Top3Entry.priority added (+ Zod schema, JSON-blob round-trip, top3 builder); OvDivergencePanel surfaces a per-entry tier badge + a cross-tier 'tier X -> Y' badge on the #1 disagreement. THRESHOLDS + canary cohort are owner-gated and NOT flipped per-workspace in this PR. (PART E, useGenQual) buildOvGainString = NON-DOLLARIZED outcome-magnitude phrase from predictedEmv (bands high>=600/med>=150/low>=1); resolveEstimatedGain overwrites all 10 estimatedGain sites in ONE post-pass chokepoint when useGenQual (flag-OFF keeps the legacy getRecoveryRate strings). content_gaps.opportunity_score recomputed in enrichment from the OV value (0..100, EMV-derived) when relaxConservatism (the same flag) so briefing-candidates + the upsell badge + the rec content_gap spine share ONE basis without breaking the cg.opportunityScore/100 spine math (owner decision (2)). estimatedGain added to stripEmvFromPublicRecs as always-on safety (sanitizes any $ exposure; it renders live at InsightsEngine.tsx). Gain renderers: FixRecommendations.tsx switched from its hand-duplicated ServerRecommendation to the shared Recommendation type (P4->P5 dependency); RecommendedForYou.tsx killed its independent volume*0.103 'est. clicks at rank #3' estimate + relabeled the /100 badge to 'Opportunity NN' (now OV-derived). (PART F) brief-cache fix (G8): buildPromptHash now keys on a tier-aware BriefRecSignal (topRecommendationId + topTier) read from the CACHED rec set, so a re-tier busts the brief cache. DECISION: hash-fix ONLY — did NOT surface the engine's #1 into the brief prompt (the brief is a distinct insight-sourced narrative surface; threading the rec set in is disproportionate), documented in docs/rules/seo-generation-quality.md. §5.2: client #1 card + AdminChat both read summary.topRecommendationId (shared basis); mcp get_pending_work answers pending client WORK not the top recommendation, so it is intentionally out-of-scope of the #1 equality (documented). Tests: tests/integration/recommendations-public-emv-leak.test.ts extended (predictedEmv strip + dollarized-gain sanitization GET+PATCH) + tests/integration/seo-genquality-p4-ov-coherence.test.ts (tier canary cross-tier move + Top3Entry.priority; one-basis parity flag-ON; flag-OFF byte-identical snapshot; brief-hash bust on re-tier; archive round-trip predicted_emv survives). Gates green: typecheck, vite build, vitest (full), pr-check, verify:feature-flags, verify:coverage-ratchet.

items.title: SEO Generation Quality P5 — keyword_gaps/topic_clusters/cannibalization as first-class recs + seoContext slice + scheduled-audit regen
items.notes: Phase 5 (final build phase) of the SEO generation-quality plan. GOLDEN RULE: all three new rec branches gate behind a single if (useGenQual) inside the existing if (strategy) block; useGenQual = isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id) resolved once per rec-gen cycle. Umbrella-OFF is byte-identical (no new recs/sources, merge + auto-resolve unchanged — proven by a flag-OFF test). NEW LITERALS (3 decisions): RecType += keyword_gap|topic_cluster|cannibalization (distinct types for the renderer maps + the exhaustive recommendationOutcomeActionType switch); ActionType += competitor_gap_closed|cluster_published|cannibalization_resolved (distinct -> honest winRateByActionType); OpportunityInput.branch REUSED (NO new branch literal): keyword_gap->ranking_opp, topic_cluster->content_gap, cannibalization->technical (computeOpportunityValue branch switch + DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS stay exhaustive; predictedEmv computed for free). THREE BRANCHES (each mirrors the content-gap shape: OV-context locals, applyRecommendationOutcomeAdjustment, legacy estimatedGain rewritten by P4's chokepoint under useGenQual): keyword_gap = listKeywordGaps(ws.id) slice(0,10) -> ranking_opp (volume/difficulty/currentPosition:competitorPosition), source RecSource.keywordGap(keyword), type keyword_gap, actionType content_creation; topic_cluster = listTopicClusters(ws.id) is coverage-ASC sorted -> clusters[0] (weakest) ONLY, EXACTLY ONE cluster-head rec NOT N, content_gap branch opportunityScore=100-coveragePercent, source RecSource.topicCluster(topic); cannibalization = listCannibalizationIssues(ws.id) -> technical (severity high->error/medium->warning/low->info, currentClicks=sum pages[].clicks), source RecSource.cannibalization(urlSetKey), actionType manual, affectedPages=pages.map(p=>p.path). FM-2/G2: each orphan read in its OWN try/catch calling failedCategories.add(<category>) on catch (mirrors decay/ctr/diagnostic/freshness) so a transient empty read does NOT drop the source from newSources and falsely bulk-auto-resolve (mark completed) prior recs. CANNIBALIZATION DEDUPE (two sites): rec-gen site = getInsights(ws.id,'cannibalization') builds active-(unresolved)-insight URL-set keys; an issue covered by an active insight does NOT mint a rec (cross-link). briefing site (G10), DEFENSIVE only (NOT load-bearing) = collectAllCandidates cross-source dedup after the collection loop drops a referenceType:'recommendation' cannibalization candidate when a matching referenceType:'analytics_insight' cannibalization candidate covers the same URL set; the real double-count guard is briefing-cron.ts dispatchableCandidates, which admits a recommendation candidate only when id.startsWith('gap-') and a cannibalization rec candidate's id is rec-<id>, so it can never become a briefing story regardless of this dedupe (kept as harmless future-proofing). Both key on cannibalizationUrlSetKey() (order-independent normalized slug join). FULL RIPPLE LOCKSTEP (one commit): compile-enforced = RecType union + recommendationOutcomeActionType exhaustive never switch (3 cases -> new ActionTypes, NOT audit_fix family) + ActionType union + DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG (outcome-scoring-defaults.ts) ScoringConfigEntry per new ActionType + recommendationSchema.type enum (workspace-schemas.ts) gains the 3 literals (WITHOUT this the new recs fail Zod validation and are SILENTLY DROPPED on every reload — found+fixed during testing, the Schema-vs-stored-shape hazard) + the 3 label maps (admin outcomeConstants, client OutcomeSummary, WinsSurface) + REC_TYPE_TAB/TYPE_ICONS (InsightsEngine.tsx). pr-check-enforced (new-rec-type-source-needs-category-and-action-type) = RecSourceCategory union + REC_SOURCE_CATEGORIES array both gain the 3 members + RecSource.keywordGap/topicCluster/cannibalization builders. grep-only = FixRecommendations.tsx typeConfig gains 3 explicit entries. INTELLIGENCE SLICE (Data-Flow #6, G7): EXTENDED seoContext (NOT a new slice) — SeoContextSlice.keywordGaps?+topicClusters? added (+ KeywordGapItem/TopicCluster import); assembleSeoContext reads listKeywordGaps/listTopicClusters (dynamic-import, graceful-degrade, assign only when non-empty); formatSeoContextSection renders both so AdminChat isn't blind. SCHEDULED-AUDIT REGEN: scheduled-audits.ts imports + calls generateRecommendations(ws.id) in its own try/catch AFTER invalidateIntelligenceCache (mirrors routes/jobs.ts); NO manual broadcast (generateRecommendations broadcasts+invalidates internally — Bridge rule #3); a regen failure never fails the audit. Tests: tests/integration/seo-genquality-p5-orphan-recs.test.ts (port 13881) — flag-ON minting (right RecType/branch/ActionType/opportunity-in-range); topic_cluster exactly-one-head given N clusters; flag-OFF byte-identical; FM-2 (throwing keyword_gap reader -> no false auto-resolve to completed); cannibalization dedupe at BOTH sites + resolved-insight does NOT suppress; ActionType mapping (new ActionTypes not audit_fix_applied) + admin label map; cannibalizationUrlSetKey order-independence; public-leak (new recs strip emv/predictedEmv/roiPerEffortDay + no dollarized gain). Gates green: typecheck, vite build, vitest (full), pr-check, verify:feature-flags, verify:coverage-ratchet. P5 ADVERSARIAL-REVIEW FIX (2026-06-03): (C1) the cannibalization rec-gen dedupe-skip (an active insight covers the URL set) now calls failedCategories.add('cannibalization') BEFORE the continue — without it, the run1->run2 transition (prior run minted a cannibalization:<key> rec; this run an active insight covers it while the issue STILL EXISTS in cannibalization_issues) made the auto-resolve loop falsely flip the prior rec to status:completed with the Auto-resolved message AND mark its pages live via updatePageState. Adding 'cannibalization' to failedCategories protects the category from the auto-resolve loop for that run (same FM-2 transient-read semantics as the catch paths), so the prior rec is carried forward (stays pending) and pages are NOT flipped to live. Only protects runs where a dedupe-skip actually occurs (a genuinely-fixed cannibalization issue still auto-resolves on a clean run; it lingers at most one extra cycle — errs safe). A RESOLVED insight does NOT trigger the skip (the insightUrlSets builder skips resolutionStatus==='resolved'), so it does not suppress. Test added to seo-genquality-p5-orphan-recs.test.ts: run1->run2 carry-forward (prior rec stays pending, pages not live) + genuine-resolve-still-works on a clean run. (M1) softened the G10 briefing-candidate dedupe claim (NON-load-bearing: briefing-cron.ts dispatchableCandidates gap- filter is the real guard since a cannibalization rec candidate is rec-<id>) in FEATURE_AUDIT.md + this note + a code comment at dedupeCannibalization; dedupe code kept as harmless future-proofing. Flag-OFF remains byte-identical (the fix only fires inside the useGenQual cannibalization branch on a dedupe-skip).

items.title: SEO Generation Quality P7.0 — thread marketId onto local keyword candidates (per-market relevance)
items.notes: Standalone prerequisite for the P7 local-SEO track (no recs/universe/OV scope started). ALWAYS-ON data-quality fix riding the existing GLOBAL 'local-seo-visibility' flag — NO new flag, NO per-workspace umbrella gating. DEFECT: local keyword candidates were generated without a marketId, so the localSeo slice's per-market logic fell back to flat top-N and a high-scoring market-A keyword could bleed into a market-B selection (~27.5% cross-market noise, the Swish case). FIX: (1) added optional marketId?: string|null to CandidateSourceSignal + LocalSeoKeywordCandidate (server/local-seo.ts), populated ONLY by market-scoped emitters in iterateLocalCandidateSignals and carried through upsertCandidate; (2) NEW market-tagged helper localVariantKeywordsByMarket(base, markets) returning {keyword, marketId} (city/state variants attributed to their originating market; the 'near me' variant carries null) — the existing flat localVariantKeywords now delegates to it, signature unchanged so its unit tests are untouched; intent-modifier variants attributed to the primary market (markets[0]); (3) added marketId?: string|null to LocalSeoSlice['candidates'] (shared/types/intelligence.ts) and stopped the slice map dropping it (local-seo-slice.ts), resolving the prior TODO(local-seo-marketId-passthrough). MARKET-AGNOSTIC sources (explicit, strategy, tracking — TrackedKeyword has no marketId, page_assignment, content_gap, 'near me') carry null/undefined; markets are NEVER fabricated. SAMPLERS (strict improvement, never a regression): stratifiedSample already bucketed by marketId when present — now real marketId data flows so multi-market workspaces get per-market prompt coverage (flat top-N only when no candidate carries a marketId or no active markets — single-market/agnostic data unchanged); selectRelevantLocalCandidates now EXCLUDES candidates from a DIFFERENT known market when the resolved target carries a marketId, keeps market-less candidates eligible, and is identical to the prior flat heuristic when the target itself is market-less. OFF behavior: assembleLocalSeo early-returns the empty-but-valid baseline before any candidate work when the flag is off or no markets exist (confirmed unchanged). Typed contract only (no Record<string,unknown>); pure in-memory passthrough, no new provider calls (honors docs/rules/local-seo-visibility.md intelligence-boundary + provider-cost rules). Tests: tests/unit/local-seo-slice-assembly.test.ts adds a Swish-like two-market fixture (Austin vs Houston, Houston scored higher to prove the bleed) asserting no cross-market bleed in either direction, that the higher-scored cross-market candidate WOULD win with marketId stripped (fix is load-bearing), that a market-less target behaves as the prior flat heuristic, and that the stratified prompt block surfaces BOTH markets even when one dominates by score — pure unit test, no port. Files: server/local-seo.ts; server/intelligence/local-seo-slice.ts; shared/types/intelligence.ts; tests/unit/local-seo-slice-assembly.test.ts; FEATURE_AUDIT.md; data/roadmap.json. Gates green: typecheck, vite build, vitest (full 1471 files/23685 tests), pr-check, verify:feature-flags, verify:coverage-ratchet.

items.title: SEO Generation Quality P7.1 — local recommendation types (service-gap/competitor-brand/not-visible) + OV local term + regen-on-refresh
items.notes: Phase 7.1 of the SEO generation-quality plan (off P7.0). Mirrors P5 (orphan-subsystem recs as first-class recs), scoped to the LOCAL surface. THREE-GATE useLocalGenQual (resolved ONCE per rec-gen cycle, threaded as a plain boolean next to useGenQual at server/recommendations.ts generateRecommendations): useGenQual (isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id)) && posture in {local,hybrid} (getLocalSeoPosture(ws.id) — NEW exported getter in local-seo.ts reading readSettings(ws).posture; LOCAL_SEO_POSTURE const already exported from shared/types/local-seo.ts) && isFeatureEnabled('local-seo-visibility') (global). When ANY gate is false -> useLocalGenQual false -> the 2c local block is skipped entirely -> ZERO local recs/sources -> merge + auto-resolve byte-identical to pre-P7.1 (proven by a posture-gated parity test across all four false-gate combos). NEW LITERALS (3 decisions): RecType += local_visibility|local_service_gap; OpportunityInput.branch += 'local' (NEW branch literal — Scope C); ActionType += local_visibility_won|local_service_added; RecSourceCategory += local_visibility|local_service_gap. THREE BRANCHES (2c block, placed AFTER the if(audit) close — local visibility is provider-snapshot-sourced, NOT audit-dependent; each in its OWN try/catch calling failedCategories.add on catch; each mints via computeOpportunityValue({branch:'local',...}) + applyRecommendationOutcomeAdjustment): B1 local_service_gap = getLocalSeoServiceGaps(ws.id) -> {serviceId,serviceLabel,starterKeywords[]}; NO native volume so OV synthesized — starterKeywords looked up in the tracked-keyword pool (getTrackedKeywords volume map) for real volume, else fall back to a grounded composite proxy (opportunityScore=60) like P5's topic_cluster; source RecSource.localServiceGap(serviceId), actionType content_creation -> local_service_added. B2 competitor-brand = getLocalSeoCompetitorBrands(ws.id) -> only those with winsAgainstClient>0 (pack appeared + client absent = the ranking signal); source RecSource.localVisibility(normalized title), localVisibilitySignal=min(1,wins/5), actionType manual -> local_visibility_won. B3 not-visible/possible-match = buildLocalSeoKeywordVisibilitySummaryByKey(ws.id), iterate per-market entries whose posture in {not_visible,possible_match} (LOCAL_SEO_VISIBILITY_POSTURE); source RecSource.localVisibility(marketId:normalizedKeyword), localVisibilitySignal 1 (not_visible) / 0.4 (possible_match), actionType manual -> local_visibility_won. DEDUPE-VS-PANEL (transient-read-safety, mirrors P5's cannibalization-vs-insight): read getLocalSeoReadModel(ws.id, true) ONCE; when the panel report state is active (has_data/ready_no_data — matching LocalSeoVisibilityPanel's render predicate), a service-gap (by serviceId) / competitor-brand (by title) already on the panel is SKIPPED + failedCategories.add(category) so the auto-resolve loop can't falsely resolve a prior local rec that migrated surfaces; when the panel is dark (needs_market/non_local/feature_disabled) the recs are the only surface so no dedupe. B3 not-visible entries are NOT per-item panel items, so they always mint. CLIENT COPY (outcome-oriented, NO purple/admin jargon — renders on client surfaces): service-gap title "You're not targeting <service> in <market>"; competitor-brand title "<competitor> keeps winning the local pack you're absent from"; not-visible title "You're not showing in the local pack for \"<keyword>\" in <market>" (+ a possible-match variant "You might be in the local pack ... but it's not confirmed"). SCOPE C (OV local term, server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts): added 'local' to the expectedClickDelta branch switch (volume->content_gap-grade demand, else grounded composite, else bounded LOCAL_PACK_CLICKDELTA proxy) + DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS.local=4 (both exhaustive Record<branch,> -> compile-forced); NEW localUrgency multiplier = 1 + LOCAL_URGENCY_MAX(0.5)*clamp01(localVisibilitySignal) folded into roiPerEffortDay alongside the reserved timing term — default/absent signal -> 0 -> 1.0 identity so non-local/flag-OFF recs are byte-identical (the timing component dimension surfaces BOTH urgency multipliers so no new OpportunityDimension ripples). localVisibilitySignal is fed ONLY from inside the gated local branches — the scorer never reads local state. SCOPE D (regen-on-local-refresh, server/local-seo.ts runLocalSeoRefreshJob): after refresh_completed + addActivity and BEFORE updateJob(done), recompute useLocalGenQual there and call generateRecommendations(workspaceId) in its own try/catch (dynamic import breaks the recommendations.ts <-> local-seo.ts cycle — readers imported statically the other way); NO manual broadcast (generateRecommendations broadcasts+invalidates internally — Bridge rule #3); a regen failure never fails the refresh job; flag-OFF/non-local -> complete no-op. FULL RIPPLE LOCKSTEP (one commit): compile-enforced = RecType union + recommendationOutcomeActionType exhaustive never switch (+2 cases -> new ActionTypes, NOT audit_fix) + ActionType union + DEFAULT_SCORING_CONFIG (Record<ActionType,>) +2 entries + the OpportunityInput.branch switch + DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS + recommendationSchema.type enum (workspace-schemas.ts) gains local_visibility|local_service_gap (CRITICAL — P5's lesson: without it the new recs are silently Zod-dropped on reload) + 3 label maps (admin outcomeConstants, client OutcomeSummary, WinsSurface, all Record<ActionType,>) + REC_TYPE_TAB/TYPE_ICONS (InsightsEngine.tsx, Record<RecType,>). pr-check-enforced (new-rec-type-source-needs-category-and-action-type) = RecSourceCategory union + REC_SOURCE_CATEGORIES array +2 + RecSource.localVisibility/localServiceGap builders. grep-only = FixRecommendations.tsx typeConfig +2 explicit entries (no fallback-to-technical). INTELLIGENCE SLICE (Data-Flow #6): LocalSeoSlice (shared/types/intelligence.ts) += serviceGaps[] + competitorBrands[]; assembleLocalSeo (local-seo-slice.ts) populates them from getLocalSeoServiceGaps/getLocalSeoCompetitorBrands (best-effort, degrade-to-empty) AND renderLocalSeoBlock renders them into effectiveLocalSeoBlock (the field formatLocalSeoSection emits) so AdminChat sees them; baseline + early-return paths carry empty arrays. Tests: tests/integration/seo-genquality-p7-1-local-recs.test.ts (port 13882) — posture-gated parity (4 false-gate combos -> zero local recs); all-gates-ON minting (right RecType/branch/ActionType, OV in 0..100, client copy present; winsAgainstClient=0 competitor does NOT mint); FM-2 (throwing service-gap reader -> no false auto-resolve); dedupe-vs-panel (active panel item suppresses gap+brand recs, B3 still mints); Scope C (local branch scores, default-0 term is identity, positive signal raises ROI, absent signal does not perturb a non-local branch); ActionType mapping + admin label map; public-leak (local recs strip emv/predictedEmv + no dollarized gain). Spies on the local readers via vi.spyOn(localSeoModule). Also updated tests/unit/local-seo-slice-assembly.test.ts mock factory (+2 readers) and the meeting-brief-generator.test.ts localSeo fixture (+2 empty fields). Files: shared/types/recommendations.ts, shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts, shared/types/intelligence.ts, server/recommendations.ts, server/local-seo.ts, server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts, server/outcome-scoring-defaults.ts, server/schemas/workspace-schemas.ts, server/intelligence/local-seo-slice.ts, src/components/admin/outcomes/outcomeConstants.ts, src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx, src/components/client/Briefing/WinsSurface.tsx, src/components/client/InsightsEngine.tsx, src/components/client/FixRecommendations.tsx, tests + FEATURE_AUDIT.md + data/roadmap.json. items.title: SEO Generation Quality P7.2 — local-intent keywords into buildKeywordUniverse (posture-gated, per-market)
items.notes: Final P7 scope (off P7.0/P7.1). Feeds local-intent keyword candidates (stored local candidates + city/state/near-me variants) into the keyword candidate universe so the strategy pool includes local terms, but ONLY for local/hybrid workspaces with the gen-quality umbrella + local-seo-visibility flags on. STORED reads only — NO synchronous provider/getLocalVisibility call in the strategy path (honors docs/rules/local-seo-visibility.md intelligence + provider-cost boundary). THE GATE (includeLocal, mirrors P7.1's three-gate): resolved ONCE in the caller (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts, next to seoGenQualityEnabled) and threaded into buildKeywordUniverse as a plain boolean — isFeatureEnabled('seo-generation-quality', ws.id) && posture in {local,hybrid} (getLocalSeoPosture(ws.id)) && isFeatureEnabled('local-seo-visibility'). Chose the includeLocal-opt over resolve-inside because the assembler already takes its umbrella gate from the caller (relaxConservatism on the eval context) and never calls isFeatureEnabled itself — only pure geo/language DB reads stay inside. When ANY gate is false (default — omitting the option == false), NO local source runs -> the pool, sourceCounts, and GenerationQuality.poolSize are byte-identical to pre-P7.2 (proven by a parity test). buildKeywordUniverse itself already runs only on the umbrella-ON path; this adds the posture + local-seo-visibility sub-gate. THE LOCAL SOURCE: NEW KEYWORD_CANDIDATE_SOURCE.LOCAL='local' member (shared/types/keyword-universe.ts). Step (9b) in the assembler folds buildLocalSeoKeywordCandidates(ws.id) (the CHEAP stored builder — page-map/strategy/tracking/content-gap signals + the city/state/near-me local_variant keywords synthesized via localVariantKeywordsByMarket) through the SAME eligible() funnel + branded/declined hard-filters + upsertKeywordPoolCandidate Map dedup as every other source, tagged source 'local'. Behind a P1-style credit-depth cap (localCandidatesLimit 50 full / 25 quick) — but since these are STORED reads (no provider cost) the cap only BOUNDS COUNT. marketId (P7.0) rides a side-map (marketByKeyword: normalizedKeyword->marketId) because the canonical pool Map value carries only {volume,difficulty,source}; it is threaded onto the NEW optional KeywordCandidate.marketId during candidate derivation. Market-agnostic local candidates + every non-local source leave marketId null — never fabricated. TYPED CONTRACT (no Record<string,unknown>). Tests: tests/unit/keyword-strategy-universe.test.ts (+4) — local-ON (a local/hybrid workspace with a page-map service keyword + Austin market surfaces a 'local'-source city variant 'cosmetic dentistry austin' carrying the Austin marketId, sourceCounts.local>0); dedup (a local candidate also proven by GSC has exactly one pool entry); parity (includeLocal:false and omitted on a LOCAL workspace add NO 'local' candidates — pool keys byte-identical, city variant absent); boundary (spies FakeSeoProvider.getLocalVisibility, asserts NEVER called while local candidates are still folded — proves stored-only). Files: shared/types/keyword-universe.ts; server/keyword-strategy-universe.ts; server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts; tests/unit/keyword-strategy-universe.test.ts; FEATURE_AUDIT.md; data/roadmap.json. Gates green: typecheck, vite build, vitest (full), pr-check, verify:feature-flags, verify:coverage-ratchet.

items.title: Event-driven regen single-flight vs cron recsInFlight guard
items.notes: Closed in PR1 of the OV cleanup sprint. Extracted a shared `server/recommendation-regen-scheduler.ts` authority so delayed keyword-strategy follow-ons and debounced opportunity-event regens now serialize through one per-workspace single-flight path. The event debounce (`opportunity-value-events`, 90s) remains intact, but once it fires it no longer bypasses the follow-on guard. Added dedicated scheduler unit coverage plus updated contract tests around the new shared runtime authority.

items.title: Post-flip cleanup — remove legacy magic scorers + the 11 outcome-adjust call sites
items.notes: Closed in PR2 of the OV cleanup sprint. Removed the legacy recommendation scorer tail from `server/recommendations.ts`: the old `computeImpactScore`/`determinePriority` helpers, the 11 `applyRecommendationOutcomeAdjustment` call sites, and the per-branch magic `impactScore` buckets no longer participate in runtime recommendation generation. Recommendation sets now sync `impactScore` from `opportunity.value`, derive `priority` from `deriveOvTier(rec)`, and resolve `estimatedGain` from the same OV basis in one canonical post-pass. Outcome learnings are no longer read in recommendation generation; calibration stays inside `computeOpportunityValue()` / `computeOvCalibration()`. Because the legacy scorer is now gone, fresh runtime `ov_divergence` rows are intentionally no longer written; the divergence panel remains available for historical/debug review only.

items.title: pr-check magic-scale guard (forbid new inline impactScore literals outside the scorer)
items.notes: Closed alongside `ov-legacy-scorer-removal-postflip`. Added pr-check rule `recommendation impactScore must flow from canonical OV scorer`, which blocks new local `impactScore` buckets/object writes in `server/recommendations.ts` unless they route through the canonical OV scoring helper (or carry an explicit hatch). Added dedicated fixture coverage plus verified-clean allowlist documentation.

Clamp + identity-gate contract stays. Needs accumulated outcome history.

UI: new 'Feature Flags' sub-tab in WorkspaceSettings (WorkspaceFeatureFlagOverrides.tsx) — Toggle per flag, source badge (Workspace override / Global override / Env var / Default), clear-override button, and inline precedence (workspace > global > env > default) showing what 'clear' inherits. items.title: P6 (DEFERRED) — semantic business-fit + GA4 conversion value + winnability (revisit only if bad-fit issues appear post-soak)
items.notes: DEFERRED by owner 2026-06-03 — let P0–P5 soak first; build ONLY if the canaried/rolled-out workspaces show genuine BAD-FIT (off-topic recommendations slipping through). Telemetry-gated by design. Scope when triggered: (1) replace lexical inferBusinessFit (server/keyword-intelligence/rules.ts) with a SEMANTIC embedding relevance score (embed business context + page corpus + candidates, cosine) — net-new embedding subsystem, the highest-risk/cost item in the plan, NONE exists today; (2) thread GA4 conversion value -> valuePerClick (server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts) which RETIRES the P4 predictedEmv CPC-proxy placeholder; (3) GSC-proven demand -> OV confidence; (4) SERP-feature crowding -> winnability discount; (5) swap calibration basis to median(realized attributed_value / predicted_emv) in server/scoring/ov-calibration.ts (P4 made predicted_emv recoverable). items.title: API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger spec)
items.source: Market prep analysis Mar 2026; revised May 2026 post-review
items.notes: 91 route files in server/routes/ with no formal API spec. Triggers to pull forward: (a) first external API consumer signed (paid agency white-label, Zapier, contractor onboarding), (b) committing to migrate response validation to Zod for other reasons (OpenAPI becomes a free byproduct), (c) hitting 'I don't know what this endpoint returns' friction during builds. Implementation: use @asteasolutions/zod-to-openapi — NOT tsoa (requires controller-class refactor incompatible with Express+middleware style) or swagger-jsdoc (hand-maintained doc blocks rot immediately). Prerequisites before start: (1) response schemas must be migrated from shared/types/ TS interfaces to Zod (currently only request schemas are Zod via validate() middleware) — this is the bulk of the work, not the spec emission, (2) per-route security tagging for the dual auth model (HMAC x-auth-token admin vs JWT client vs public token), (3) pr-check rule asserting every Express route has a registered OpenAPI entry to prevent drift (zod-to-openapi does NOT introspect the route table — registration is opt-in per route). Out of scope: WebSocket broadcasts (would need separate AsyncAPI spec). Deliverables: registered request+response schemas per route, /api/docs Swagger UI gated to authenticated admins only, generated spec in CI artifact, drift-detection test.

sprints.name: Intelligence Quality Consolidation — Recommendations & AI Content
sprints.rationale: Close the root quality gaps in platform insights and AI content generation by joining mature subsystems through the existing intelligence conventions instead of hand-rolling context, scoring, narrative, and evidence handling per feature. The target architecture is: workspace data -> intelligence slices -> shared generation context -> AI operation contract -> deterministic recommendation/story output -> tracked action/outcome -> future intelligence. The remaining keyword work extends this consolidation into a full keyword operating loop: provider source data -> shared keyword intelligence -> strategy/recommendation judgment -> rank-tracking propagation -> content/action follow-through -> outcome feedback.

items.title: Unified signal-to-story registry for insights, briefings, and recommendations
items.source: Platform insights + AI content audit discussion 2026-05-18
items.notes: Completed in PR4. Added `server/signal-story-registry.ts` as the server-local boundary for signal→story projection across three surfaces: client insight narratives, deterministic briefing insight dispatch, and overlapping recommendation copy. `server/insight-narrative.ts` now consumes the registry instead of hand-writing inline narrative maps, which closed generic fallback gaps for `audit_finding`, `site_health`, and `milestone_attribution` while keeping explicit non-client exclusions for `strategy_alignment` and `keyword_cluster`. `server/briefing-templates/index.ts` now routes insight/content-gap dispatch through the shared registry boundary without replacing the existing deterministic templates. `server/recommendations.ts` now uses centralized story copy builders for content gaps, ranking opportunities, content decay, CTR underperformance, and freshness alerts so overlapping signals stop inventing separate phrasing. items.title: Make outcome learning a first-class recommendation/content input

Verification added focused unit/integration coverage for builder availability, content prompt fallback, recommendation scoring, and consumer-inventory drift. No production flag flip was included.
