items.title: Reduce noisy keyword recommendations and strengthen business-fit ranking
items.source: Follow-up from platform insights audit / user report 2026-05-18
items.notes: Completed in PR3 on 2026-05-18. Keyword recommendations now use `buildRecommendationGenerationContext()` with `seoContext + learnings + clientSignals`, making the shared intelligence-builder path canonical for recommendation ranking instead of the old hybrid low-level path. Smart ranking is now available behind the existing `useAI` opt-in contract, preserving route compatibility while still degrading safely to deterministic scoring if AI or context building fails. Candidate quality improved at the root: previously declined terms are suppressed, cannibalization + existing page-map conflicts are down-ranked, broad adjacent terms get penalized, requested/business-priority/recent-interest terms get boosted, and optional `includeReasoning` explainability is available for admin/debug consumers. Coverage now includes deterministic fallback, smart-path AI ranking, declined-term suppression, self-conflict exclusion for cell recommendations, duplicate-seed preservation, cannibalization down-ranking, GSC enrichment preservation, and route-level reasoning contract checks.

items.title: Keyword system source-data and lifecycle audit
items.notes:
Completed in PR9 on 2026-05-19. Added `docs/superpowers/audits/2026-05-19-keyword-operating-loop-source-lifecycle-audit.md` as the source/lifecycle audit for the keyword operating loop. The audit maps current keyword sources, consumers, mutation paths, strategy refresh follow-ons, rank-tracking behavior, strategy-history diffs, client feedback, outcome tracking, broadcasts/cache invalidation, and source-to-surface handoffs.

Key findings:
- The platform already has the bones of a keyword operating loop: strategy persistence writes page keywords/history/table-backed strategy data, seeds strategy keywords into rank tracking, queues llms.txt regeneration, refreshes recommendations, broadcasts `STRATEGY_UPDATED`, and invalidates intelligence caches.
- The loop is still lifecycle-thin: rank tracking is append-only (`query`, `pinned`, `addedAt`), strategy refresh does not reconcile stale/reassigned/replaced strategy-owned keywords, tracked keywords do not preserve source/page/strategy/baseline metadata, and outcome tracking currently records one strategy-level `strategy_keyword_added` action with `targetKeyword: null`.
- Client feedback is already meaningful input for strategy and recommendations, but requested/approved/declined terms should become first-class lifecycle constraints in the shared keyword engine and tracking reconciliation.
- DataForSEO coverage is useful but narrow. Current wrappers cover search volume, keyword difficulty, related keywords, question-filtered keyword suggestions, ranked keywords, competitors, synthesized keyword gap, and backlinks. Missing/underused PR10 candidates include `keyword_ideas/live`, `keywords_for_site/live`, non-question `keyword_suggestions/live`, Google Ads `keywords_for_keywords/live`, `domain_intersection/live`, `page_intersection/live`, and `keyword_overview/live`.

Sequencing confirmed:
1. PR10 should expand keyword source data through provider capabilities and normalized source evidence.
2. PR11 should add strategy refresh propagation and rank-tracking reconciliation.
3. PR12 should introduce the shared keyword-intelligence engine for recommendations and strategy judgment.
4. PR13 should add the strategy quality, explainability, and action-loop UX pass.

No production behavior changes were included in the audit PR.

items.title: Keyword source-data expansion through DataForSEO capabilities
items.notes:
Completed in PR10 on 2026-05-19. Expanded the keyword source-data layer through the provider abstraction so strategy generation can pull richer discovery candidates without importing DataForSEO directly or rewriting ranking/scoring yet.

What changed:
- Added shared normalized keyword source evidence types in `shared/types/keywords.ts` with source-kind metadata for DataForSEO Labs keyword ideas, keywords-for-site, general keyword suggestions, Google Ads keywords-for-keywords, and existing keyword source families.
- Extended `SeoDataProvider` with optional capability-aware discovery methods: `getKeywordIdeas()`, `getKeywordsForSite()`, `getKeywordSuggestions()`, and `getKeywordsForKeywords()`.
- Implemented those capabilities in `DataForSeoProvider` with bounded seed counts, capped result limits, workspace file-cache keys, discovery TTLs, credit usage labels, malformed/empty-response tolerance, and endpoint-level failure fallback to `[]`.
- Wired discovery collection into keyword strategy source gathering only as additional candidate availability. Full-mode strategy generation now attempts site, ideas, and general suggestions when provider capabilities exist, dedupes normalized candidates, and adds a bounded discovery block to SEO context. Google Ads keywords-for-keywords remains implemented at the provider layer, but a staging QA hotfix on 2026-05-19 stopped feeding that source into strategy generation by default because planner-style grouped volumes produced noisy high-volume page-assignment candidates; PR12 should reintroduce it only behind shared engine quality gates if it proves useful. The same staging QA pass also corrected topical-authority coverage semantics so service pages and strategy page-map assignments count as coverage even when the site does not yet rank for the exact provider keyword; average position remains ranking-data-only.
- Threaded discovery candidates into strategy synthesis as extra keyword-pool inputs tagged by source kind, while preserving the existing orchestration and final ranking/scoring behavior for PR12.

Quality and guardrails:
- Provider-specific response shapes stay inside `server/providers/dataforseo-provider.ts`; strategy modules consume only `SeoDataProvider` and normalized `KeywordSourceEvidence`.
- Current strategy generation degrades safely if any discovery endpoint fails, credits are exhausted, or the provider lacks a capability.
- Focused tests cover DataForSEO request payloads/normalization for the new endpoints and a cold-start-ish strategy source-layer case where discovery candidates exist even when domain ranked keywords are thin.

Follow-on sequencing remains unchanged:
1. PR11 should reconcile strategy refreshes into rank tracking and downstream keyword lifecycle state.
2. PR12 should introduce the shared keyword-intelligence engine for aligned filtering/scoring/explainability.
3. PR13 should surface strategy quality, explanations, and action-loop UX.

Staging functional-audit hotfix follow-up on 2026-05-19:
- Tightened strategy keyword-pool source precedence so stronger provider/competitor/gap evidence can upgrade earlier GSC-only candidates instead of being shadowed by insertion order.
- Added a quality gate for provider discovery candidates entering strategy synthesis: discovery terms must have usable demand and difficulty so unknown/zero-quality candidates do not drive page assignments or content gaps.
- Corrected content-gap and topical-authority semantics: mapped pages/titles count as coverage, but generic assigned keywords do not claim location/cost long-tail coverage; content gaps already covered by existing mapped pages are removed before scoring.
- Hardened declined/blank keyword handling so empty primary keywords do not match every GSC query or produce blank cannibalization groups.
- Made strategy export read the normalized page_keywords table, not the legacy strategy blob pageMap.
- Stopped strategy persistence from stamping Page Intelligence analysis freshness. Existing Page Intelligence analysis is preserved only when the primary keyword remains the same; if strategy regeneration changes the primary keyword, stale analysis fields are cleared so the UI does not imply the old analysis applies to the new keyword.
- Updated topical-authority and client keyword-map copy to be provider-neutral and clearer about mixed coverage vs ranking-only average position.

Deferred by design to PR11/PR12:
- Rank tracking remains append-only until the dedicated strategy refresh propagation/reconciliation PR.
- Strategy diff visibility for topic clusters, quick wins, keyword gaps, and cannibalization remains part of the explainability/shared-engine follow-up work.

items.title: Shared keyword-intelligence engine for strategy generation and recommendations
items.source: Follow-up from strategy-generation vs keyword-recommendation architecture discussion 2026-05-19
items.notes:
Create the shared keyword-intelligence substrate that both keyword strategy generation and keyword recommendations consume, without collapsing them into one end-to-end generator. This item should start after the source/lifecycle audit and, ideally, after the provider source-data expansion has defined normalized candidate evidence. It should happen before the final strategy UX/quality pass and can run before or alongside rank-tracking reconciliation only if contracts are locked first.

Problem to solve:
- The platform currently has adjacent but separate keyword systems: full-site strategy generation (`server/keyword-strategy-generation.ts`, `server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts`, jobs/routes) and narrower keyword recommendations/ranking (`server/keyword-recommendations.ts`).
- These systems should share business-fit judgment, client-feedback constraints, candidate suppression, cannibalization/page-map awareness, authority posture, source evidence, and explainability.
- They should remain separate top-level orchestrators because strategy generation is a long-running background synthesis workflow while recommendations are faster recommendation/reranking workflows.

Target architecture:
- Keep `keyword-strategy-generation` and `keyword-recommendations` as separate top-level orchestrators.
- Introduce a server-local keyword-intelligence module underneath them with five layers: (1) shared keyword context builder(s) built on the intelligence conventions, (2) shared source/candidate normalization contracts from provider/GSC/client/strategy inputs, (3) shared candidate filtering and suppression rules, (4) shared scoring/evaluation utilities, and (5) shared reasoning/explainability contracts.
- Mirror the builder consolidation philosophy already used for AI context: unify inputs/contracts/decision substrate, not every product surface.

Scope details:
- Add a server-local keyword-intelligence bounded-context module that owns reusable keyword context assembly for both strategy and recommendation flows. Minimum shared inputs should include `seoContext`, `learnings`, `clientSignals`, existing `page_keywords` / page map state, content gaps, cannibalization issues, requested keywords, declined keywords, brand/business-context constraints, authority posture when available, and normalized keyword source evidence from provider/GSC/client inputs.
- Extract shared candidate filters so the same rules are not reimplemented in multiple places: exact/near-duplicate suppression, declined-keyword suppression, requested-keyword boosts, client-approved keyword preservation, brand/offering mismatch checks, obvious low-actionability/noise filters, cannibalization negatives, existing-page-map conflicts, and authority mismatch posture.
- Extract shared judgment/scoring helpers where business rules should stay aligned: business-fit score, intent-match score, authority-adjusted difficulty classification, opportunity scoring, source-evidence confidence, keyword-owner/source-lifecycle weighting, and page-target conflict severity.
- Add a shared explanation contract for admin/debug use: why a keyword was selected, why it was rejected/down-ranked, what evidence supported it, what constraints applied, whether it is tracked, and whether the ranking came from deterministic scoring, AI reranking, or a hybrid path.
- Migrate keyword recommendations onto the shared engine first because the blast radius is smaller and UI-visible validation is easier.
- Migrate strategy synthesis/filtering paths onto the same shared engine in a behavior-preserving pass, then tighten strategy generation quality only after parity is covered.

Post-PR11 pre-implementation amendments:
- Add an explicit consumer/cache/broadcast contract checkpoint before implementation. PR11 showed that keyword lifecycle state is consumed by more surfaces than the obvious strategy/recommendation screens: Rank Tracker, Page Intelligence badges, admin Strategy badges, client Strategy, workspace home, admin chat/intelligence context, feedback approvals, page keyword tables, recommendation refresh, and strategy-history/diff readers. PR12 should map these consumers and their cache/event expectations before migrating shared rules so visible surfaces do not drift.
- Add concrete noisy-term regression fixtures from staging before changing scoring: for hmpsn studio-like business context, terms such as `paper tiger` and `typing tiger` should be rejected or heavily down-ranked because they have weak business fit despite lexical overlap. Capture the typed reason as business/offering mismatch or low actionability rather than treating them as generic duplicates.
- Separate deterministic correctness tests from subjective quality tests. Correctness fixtures should be hard gates for declined suppression, duplicate/near-duplicate handling, client-requested preservation, page-map conflicts, source-evidence preservation, and fallback behavior. Quality fixtures should assert relative/posture outcomes for noisy terms, business-fit mismatches, and authority ambition without overfitting exact final scores.
- Keep the first strategy migration behavior-preserving except for explicitly approved shared-engine quality gates. Any intentional behavior change, such as suppressing `paper tiger` / `typing tiger`, needs a named fixture and typed explanation so we can tell real improvements from accidental ranking drift.
- Require review checklists to include data-flow/cache/broadcast drift, strategy-vs-recommendation divergence, provider-contract leakage, AI/provider/context fallback behavior, and legacy keyword compatibility. This is now part of the PR12 entry criteria, not a late review surprise.

Non-goals:
- Do not merge keyword strategy generation and keyword recommendations into one giant function or one route/job type.
- Do not use this PR as a dumping ground for every DataForSEO endpoint addition; source-data expansion should already be isolated or explicitly contracted.
- Do not force an AI-only solution; deterministic filters/scoring must remain usable when AI is off or unavailable.
- Do not silently change live strategy generation behavior without representative parity tests.
- Do not auto-publish or auto-apply content/site changes from keyword intelligence decisions.

Suggested sequencing when picked up:
1. Consume the keyword source/lifecycle audit, provider source-data contracts, and PR11 reconciliation lessons.
2. Map all current strategy/recommendation/rank-tracking/page-keyword/intelligence consumers and lock cache/broadcast expectations before implementation.
3. Define server-local contracts first: shared context input type, normalized candidate record shape, evaluation result shape, lifecycle/source-evidence shape, and optional explanation primitives.
4. Extract deterministic shared utilities without materially changing output ordering except for named quality-gate fixtures.
5. Migrate recommendations onto the shared engine first.
6. Migrate strategy candidate filtering/pool construction and page-assignment guardrails onto the shared engine in a behavior-preserving pass.
7. Add parity tests for equivalent recommendation-vs-strategy scenarios, including staging-observed noise fixtures.
8. Add guardrails/tests so future keyword features cannot bypass the shared engine without an explicit hatch.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both keyword strategy generation and keyword recommendations consume the same shared keyword-intelligence rules for client feedback constraints, duplicate suppression, page-map/cannibalization awareness, source evidence, authority posture, and business-fit evaluation.
- Recommendation and strategy paths can diverge at orchestration level while still producing aligned keyword judgment in equivalent scenarios.
- Shared explanations can answer, at least in admin/debug contexts: why selected, why suppressed, why downgraded, source evidence, tracking/lifecycle posture, and deterministic-vs-AI ranking path.
- Tests cover deterministic shared filters, strategy-vs-recommendation parity on representative inputs, declined/requested/approved keyword handling, cannibalization/page-conflict handling, source-evidence preservation, authority posture, safe fallback when learnings/authority/source expansion are absent, and observed noisy-term fixtures such as `paper tiger` / `typing tiger` for hmpsn studio-like context.
- The PR includes an explicit consumer/cache/broadcast map before implementation, and review notes verify no visible keyword/rank/intelligence consumer is left stale by the shared-engine migration.
- Documentation explicitly states the boundary: separate orchestrators, shared engine underneath.

Completed in PR12 on 2026-05-20. Added a server-local shared keyword-intelligence engine under `server/keyword-intelligence/` and migrated the first two high-value consumers onto it without collapsing their separate orchestrators.

What changed:
- Centralized deterministic keyword rules for normalization, near-duplicate matching, candidate inclusion thresholds, opportunity scoring, declined/requested/approved feedback handling, business-fit posture, page-map conflict detection, authority mismatch reasoning, and typed suppression/down-ranking reasons.
- Migrated `server/keyword-recommendations.ts` so recommendation candidates are evaluated through the shared engine while preserving the existing optional reasoning response shape and AI reranking fallback.
- Added a shared strategy-pool eligibility gate in `server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts` so provider, competitor, gap, discovery, and related candidates are screened before entering the AI strategy prompt.
- Added the PR12 consumer/cache/broadcast contract audit at `docs/superpowers/audits/2026-05-20-pr12-keyword-intelligence-consumer-contract.md`, explicitly mapping strategy/recommendation/rank/page/intelligence consumers before implementation.
- Added focused fixtures for staging-observed noisy terms `paper tiger` and `typing tiger`, which are now suppressed for hmpsn studio-like business context with typed noise/business-fit reasons.

Scope boundaries preserved:
- Strategy generation and keyword recommendations remain separate top-level orchestrators.
- PR12 does not introduce client API changes, publishing behavior, or a broad UI explainability pass.
- Provider-specific payloads remain outside the shared engine; source expansion remains isolated behind provider/source collection modules.

Follow-on:
- PR13 should surface selected/suppressed/down-ranked keyword explanations, strategy refresh summaries, tracking status, and next-action UX using the typed reasons now produced by the shared engine.

items.title: Keyword strategy refresh propagation and rank-tracking reconciliation
items.notes:
Completed in PR11 on 2026-05-19. Strategy regeneration now reconciles rank tracking intentionally instead of only append-seeding keywords.

What changed:
- Added shared rank-tracking lifecycle contracts in `shared/types/rank-tracking.ts` with optional source/status/page/strategy/baseline metadata while preserving backward compatibility for old `{ query, pinned, addedAt }` rows.
- Added `server/rank-tracking-reconciliation.ts` to compare the new strategy against existing tracked keywords and produce a typed change set: added, retained, reassigned, deprecated, replaced, manually preserved, and skipped.
- Strategy-owned keywords are now updated with page assignment, strategy timestamps, intent, provider metrics, and baseline rank/click/impression fields when available.
- Removed strategy-owned keywords are marked `deprecated`; page-owned strategy keywords whose page receives a different primary keyword are marked `replaced` with `replacedBy` metadata.
- Manual, client-requested, recommendation/content-gap, unknown, and pinned keywords are preserved safely so regeneration does not clobber human-selected tracking.
- Admin rank-tracking add/remove/pin/snapshot routes now broadcast `RANK_TRACKING_UPDATED` and add activity entries, aligning admin behavior with the richer public/client mutation flow.
- Strategy reconciliation broadcasts rank-tracking changes, records a summarized activity entry, and invalidates workspace intelligence/SEO context so downstream consumers see updated tracked keyword state.
- Frontend event handling now refreshes Rank Tracker, Page Intelligence tracking badges, Keyword Strategy tracking badges, and relevant React Query caches on rank-tracking and strategy events.

Quality and compatibility:
- Existing callers that only read `query`, `pinned`, and `addedAt` continue to work because all new fields are optional.
- Legacy tracked keyword JSON rows normalize to `source: unknown` and `status: active` at the read boundary.
- Focused tests cover lifecycle reconciliation, backward-compatible row parsing, latest-rank metadata, and follow-on wiring.

Deferred by design:
- PR12 should move keyword judgment/scoring/filter rules into the shared keyword intelligence engine.
- PR13 should surface the strategy refresh change set and keyword-level explanations in admin/client UX.
- Local SEO remains a follow-on roadmap item and should not be folded into this reconciliation PR beyond future-safe optional lifecycle fields.

items.title: Keyword strategy refactor and signal hygiene
items.source: Staging QA and keyword strategy refactor discussion 2026-05-20
items.notes: Completed in this PR. Added a final deterministic hygiene boundary for keyword strategy outputs before persistence and rank-tracking reconciliation. The new sanitizer applies the PR12 shared keyword-intelligence rules to selected page primaries, secondary keywords, site keywords, and content-gap targets after synthesis and again after enrichment, repairing page primaries through an explicit fallback order and dropping unrecoverable blank/noisy mappings instead of persisting invalid keywords. Strategy-owned stale/noisy tracked keywords are retired through the existing PR11 lifecycle because sanitized outputs no longer include those terms, while manual, pinned, client-requested, content-gap, and recommendation-owned keywords remain preserved. Strategy Signals now use the shared keyword-intelligence context when available, suppressing noisy keyword-derived persisted insights at the signal/prompt boundary without deleting or auto-resolving raw analytics_insights history. This PR intentionally preserves route/client payload compatibility and does not implement the PR13 UX/action-loop surface or local SEO.

items.title: Keyword strategy quality, explainability, and action loop UX pass
items.notes:
After source data, shared intelligence, and propagation are in place, make the keyword strategy experience visibly more useful to admins and clients. This is the PR13 quality/UX pass that turns the backend operating loop into product value.

Intent:
- Move the platform from “we generated a keyword strategy” to “here is the keyword operating plan, why each keyword was chosen, how it is performing, what changed, and what to do next.”

Implementation opportunities:
- Add a strategy refresh summary: keywords added, removed/deprecated, reassigned to new pages, preserved because manually pinned/client-requested, and content gaps newly created or resolved.
- Add keyword-level explainability using the shared keyword intelligence explanation primitives: source evidence, business fit, client feedback, existing page conflict/cannibalization posture, authority posture, opportunity score, and whether AI reranking or deterministic scoring influenced the choice.
- Improve admin surfaces around page keyword maps: show why each page has its primary keyword, whether it came from GSC/provider/client request/competitor gap/source expansion, and whether the platform considers it reachable now or authority-building.
- Improve rank-tracking and Page Intelligence handoff: show newly tracked keywords, tracking status, baseline state, and whether rank data is waiting for the next snapshot.
- Improve client Strategy tab copy without overexposing internals: frame strategy changes as “what changed,” “what we are watching,” and “what we recommend doing next.”
- Connect keyword choices to content actions: generate brief CTA for new content gaps, optimization CTA for existing-page keyword moves, and recommendation card refreshes for newly underperforming or newly reachable opportunities.
- Preserve no-publishing/no-auto-action posture: this PR should explain and queue actions, not silently publish content or change live site metadata.

Tests:
- Component/hook tests for refresh summary and keyword explanation rendering if UI changes land.
- Contract tests ensuring explanation payloads remain optional/backward-compatible if exposed through existing APIs.
- Integration tests proving a regenerated strategy can produce a coherent change set and that deprecated/reassigned keywords render safely.
- Staging QA on at least two workspaces: an established site with rich GSC/provider data and a sparse/cold-start site.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admins can quickly answer: why this keyword, what changed, what should we track, and what action should happen next.
- Clients see a clearer strategy story without raw-provider jargon.
- Keyword choices are connected to content briefs/recommendations/rank tracking in the UI.
- No client API breakage and no automatic publishing behavior is introduced.

Prerequisite now satisfied by `intel-quality-keyword-strategy-refactor-signal-hygiene`: PR13 can build UX/explainability on sanitized strategy outputs, lifecycle-retired stale tracking keywords, and signal-boundary-filtered keyword insights rather than compensating for noisy backend primitives.

Completed in PR13 on 2026-05-20. Added the visible strategy quality/action-loop layer on top of the PR10-PR12 source, lifecycle, and shared keyword-intelligence work.

What changed:
- Added shared optional strategy UX contracts in `shared/types/keyword-strategy-ux.ts` for refresh summaries, keyword explanations, tracking/action state, and raw evidence posture.
- Added `server/keyword-strategy-ux.ts`, a read-only assembler that derives explanations from existing strategy, page keyword, content-gap, keyword-gap, feedback, rank-tracking lifecycle, and shared keyword-intelligence evaluation data.
- Extended admin strategy reads, strategy diff reads, and public/client strategy reads backward-compatibly with optional `strategyUx` / summary / explanation fields.
- Upgraded admin "What Changed" into a refresh summary with added/retained/reassigned/retired/preserved counts and explanation previews.
- Relabeled competitor keyword gaps as raw provider evidence so noisy provider terms are not presented as selected strategy actions.
- Added client-safe strategy refresh and explanation affordances in the Strategy tab, keyword drawer, and page keyword map, preserving existing feedback/request flows.
- Added small rank-tracker handoff labels for strategy/client-sourced tracked keywords.

Scope boundaries preserved:
- No scoring/ranking rewrite, no automatic regeneration, no publishing/live metadata writes, no DB migration, and no local SEO implementation.
- Optional fields are additive and absent-safe for existing callers.

Verification coverage:
- Added focused tests for refresh summary counts, keyword explanation mapping, raw evidence posture, and client-safe omission of competitor-gap evidence.

items.title: Keyword Command Center operating surface
items.notes:
Completed in this PR on 2026-05-20. Added a dedicated admin Keywords surface at `/ws/:workspaceId/seo-keywords` so keyword lifecycle management has one operating layer instead of being split across Strategy, Rank Tracker, Page Intelligence, and client feedback surfaces.

What changed:
- Added shared command-center contracts for keyword rows, lifecycle statuses, source/evidence labels, metrics, tracking state, feedback state, safe next actions, counts, filters, and action payloads.
- Added a minimal shared canonical keyword comparison helper for command-center joins and touched keyword lifecycle comparisons. Broader normalization migration remains the immediate follow-up item.
- Added `GET /api/webflow/keyword-command-center/:workspaceId`, assembling strategy site/page/content terms, raw competitor/provider evidence, tracked keywords including inactive lifecycle rows, keyword feedback, PR13 explanations, and latest rank evidence into one capped read model.
- Added `POST /api/webflow/keyword-command-center/:workspaceId/actions` for `add_to_strategy`, `promote_evidence`, `track`, `pause_tracking`, `retire`, `decline`, and `restore`. These actions preserve rank history, use lifecycle status rather than hard delete, protect manual/pinned/client-requested keywords from accidental retirement, broadcast strategy/rank/intelligence changes where relevant, and log admin activity.
- Added the admin `Keywords` route/nav/command-palette/breadcrumb wiring and a dense Command Center UI with summary cards, filters/search, table rows, a sticky keyword detail drawer, evidence labels, feedback/tracking posture, and safe handoffs to Strategy, Rank Tracker, Page Intelligence, and content planning. No publishing or live metadata writes were added.

Follow-on:
- The existing `intel-quality-keyword-normalization-route-reliability-hardening` item now follows immediately after this work and before local SEO so the wider keyword loop can converge on one canonical equality contract and safer route failure handling.

items.title: Keyword Command Center latency hardening + strategy aux-load split follow-up
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-27 as the closeout for the two-phase Keywords latency plan. Phase 1: kept the `/seo-keywords` shell instant by letting rows drive first paint while summary and local-visibility sections remain explicitly deferred/skeleton-backed, and kept Strategy analysis non-blocking when provider/workspace aux metadata is still loading. Phase 2: tightened the KCC backend read path by extending lightweight page-keyword reads to the detail builder path as well (`listPageKeywordsLite` for keyword page-map assignment + demand fields only), preserving response contract shapes and lifecycle/filter/action behavior. Added regression coverage for summary-vs-rows filter parity, lightweight page-keyword read output boundaries, and Strategy aux-loading non-blocking UI behavior. No API contract breaks, no schema migration, and no publishing/live metadata writes.

items.title: Keyword normalization and route reliability hardening
items.source: PR13 + Keyword Command Center follow-up before local SEO 2026-05-20
items.notes: Completed in PR15 on 2026-05-20. Promoted the Command Center keyword comparison helper into the shared keyword-loop equality contract and migrated high-value semantic keyword joins to `keywordComparisonKey()` / `normalizeKeywordForComparison()` while preserving raw display strings and provider request payloads. Covered Strategy, Rank Tracker, rank-tracking reconciliation, Page Keywords, Keyword Command Center, keyword recommendations, strategy enrichment/synthesis, content-pipeline coverage gaps, client Strategy feedback helpers, keyword gaps, cannibalization issues, and provider metric joins. Added `docs/rules/keyword-normalization-route-hardening.md` to document when canonical keyword equality is appropriate versus raw keyword strings. Tightened touched async route handling for high-value keyword-loop routes so snapshot/signal failures flow through explicit Express error handling. Added regression coverage for punctuation/case/spacing variants such as `Emergency Dentist - Near-Me` matching `emergency dentist near me`, duplicate rank-tracking prevention, strategy reconciliation retention, and Command Center lifecycle actions across canonical variants. Local pack visibility can now build on one keyword equality contract instead of reintroducing city/near-me duplicate bugs.

items.title: Local SEO foundation, source, and market audit
items.notes:
Completed in PR14 on 2026-05-20. Added `docs/superpowers/audits/2026-05-20-local-seo-foundation-source-market-audit.md` and `docs/rules/local-seo-visibility.md` to ground local SEO before provider implementation. The audit ran a read-only foundation sniff across Strategy, Keywords, Rank Tracker, Page Intelligence, Client Strategy, schema LocalBusiness evidence, business profile contact data, existing `local_pack` SERP feature preservation, and intelligence prompt boundaries. No production behavior, route, DB, or client payload changes were included.

Key findings:
- The keyword operating loop is sufficiently clean for local SEO planning: declined terms are suppressed, retired strategy-owned tracked keywords are hidden from active rank views, raw provider evidence is labeled separately, Strategy Signals use the shared keyword-intelligence boundary, and Rank Tracker remains GSC measurement-only.
- The main prerequisite before provider implementation is still the pending `intel-quality-keyword-normalization-route-reliability-hardening` item. Local SEO will multiply city/neighborhood/near-me variants, so PR15 should wait for that hardening PR or explicitly share its normalizer contract.
- Existing business profile contact fields and schema LocalBusiness paths provide useful match evidence, but the platform has no explicit workspace local posture, service-area objects, local market model, or GBP identity yet.
- Existing `local_pack` SERP feature data is preserved as feature evidence, but it is not market-specific local visibility.
- DataForSEO supports the likely v1 sources through Google Organic SERP local-pack items, Google Local Finder, and Google Maps SERP; Google Business Data and Reviews are better follow-on items.

PR15 should add bounded, feature-flagged local pack visibility storage/provider contracts behind a background job. PR16 should surface admin-first reporting and only add client-facing local summaries after staging data quality is validated.

items.title: Local pack visibility foundation
items.notes:
Completed in PR16 on 2026-05-20. Added the bounded, feature-flagged local-pack visibility foundation after PR15 keyword equality hardening.

What changed:
- Added shared local SEO contracts in `shared/types/local-seo.ts` for workspace posture, posture source, markets, devices, provider source endpoints, local visibility snapshots, business-match confidence, and degraded/provider-failure states.
- Added the `local-seo-visibility` feature flag and `local-seo-refresh` background job type.
- Added storage via `096-local-seo-visibility.sql` for workspace local SEO settings, explicit local markets, and append-only local visibility snapshots separate from GSC Rank Tracker snapshots.
- Extended `SeoDataProvider` with optional `getLocalVisibility()` and implemented DataForSEO Google Organic SERP local-pack parsing inside the provider layer, with local endpoint credit labels, cache keys that include keyword/market/device/language/source endpoint, typed provider-failure degradation, and no direct DataForSEO imports in strategy/UI code.
- Added server-local local SEO service logic for posture suggestions, business-profile market suggestions, explicit admin market storage, local-intent keyword selection from active tracked/page-assigned terms, conservative business match confidence, snapshot persistence, broadcasts, activity logging, and intelligence cache invalidation.

Refreshes are gated by `local-seo-visibility`, run through a background job, originally capped v1 to three markets and twenty-five keywords, and do not mutate strategy, publish content, write metadata, or mix local visibility into Rank Tracker.

Scope boundaries preserved: no client-facing local SEO payloads, no reporting UI, no GBP health/reviews, no geo-grid tracking, no synchronous strategy-generation provider fanout, and no publishing/live-site mutation. items.title: Local keyword lens and visibility usability
items.source: Local SEO staging QA and Keyword Command Center usability discussion 2026-05-20
items.notes:
Completed in this PR on 2026-05-20. Made Local SEO Visibility operate as a lens inside the Keyword Command Center instead of a separate keyword silo.

What changed:

Declined, retired, duplicate, weak-fit, and known-noisy candidates are suppressed through the shared keyword-intelligence rules.

items.title: Keyword Command Center OOM hotfix
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. Staging showed `/seo-keywords` could still trigger Render 502s on rich workspaces because the split Command Center endpoints were still sharing a heavy full-universe builder and local SEO candidate generation could grow a large intermediate candidate map before output caps applied. The hotfix keeps public route and payload shapes unchanged while making `/summary` scalar/count-only, gating local candidate generation behind only the explicit Local Candidates filter for `/rows`, keeping aggregate Local / Not Checked filters snapshot-only, adding a hard internal local-candidate cap of 1000, and logging heap/timing fields for Command Center read-model builds. Local candidate counts are intentionally conservative in the skinny summary until the user requests a local candidate view. No publishing, metadata writes, schema writes, GBP mutation, or strategy regeneration were added.

items.title: Keyword Command Center skinny endpoint architecture
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21 as Phase 2 of the Keyword Command Center crash-hotfix plan. The split Command Center endpoints no longer hide the full-universe read model behind normal app navigation: `/summary` remains scalar/count-only, non-local-candidate `/rows` uses a skinny source-bundle path with `rows-skinny` timing logs, and `/detail` assembles only the requested canonical keyword with `detail-skinny` timing logs. The legacy full `GET /api/webflow/keyword-command-center/:workspaceId` endpoint, API helper, and unused React Query hook were removed so new callers cannot accidentally rebuild the whole keyword universe. Added pr-check guardrails that prevent summary/detail from calling `buildKeywordCommandCenterModel()` and prevent ungated local SEO candidate generation in Command Center read paths. Local Candidates intentionally remains on the explicit capped temporary path until the follow-on local candidate-cache work lands. No publishing, metadata writes, schema writes, GBP mutation, geo-grid tracking, local strategy regeneration, or local candidate DB cache was added.

items.title: Keyword Command Center row expansion + localCandidates count fix
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. Staging showed filtered tabs inflating beyond their badge counts (Tracked badge 235 / table 275; In Strategy 224 / table 227; Visible Locally 1 / table showed many non-local rows with `—` posture). Root cause: `filterBundleToKeys` and `buildFilteredBundle` used a boolean `pageMatchesKeys` that let pages survive whole if ANY of their primary/secondary keywords was in the selected key set, after which `populateDraftRows` created rows for every page keyword — inflating filtered results with sibling keywords. Fix: introduced `restrictPageToKeys` transformer that trims each surviving page's primary and secondary keywords to only those in the key set (or drops the page if nothing remains), making the paged key set a hard row-creation boundary. Added post-materialization filter validation in `buildKeywordCommandCenterRowsSkinny` as defense-in-depth with a `rowsDropped` diagnostic in the structured log line so regressions surface in staging logs. Also fixed `localCandidates: 0` hardcoded in the skinny summary so the badge reflects the bounded candidate generator output (still capped by `LOCAL_CANDIDATE_HARD_CAP = 1000`). Added 6 regression tests covering tracked/empty/visible_locally/possible_match/all-count-alignment/localCandidates-badge cases. No public route or payload shape changes. **NOTE 2026-05-21:** localCandidates count portion of this PR caused a 35-second summary regression on rich workspaces and was reverted in `intel-quality-localcandidates-summary-revert`. Other parts (row expansion fix) remain in place.

items.title: Revert localCandidates summary count (35s regression on Swish)
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. The localCandidates count addition from PR #876 ran `buildLocalSeoKeywordCandidates(workspace.id).length` on every summary request. Although the candidates Map was bounded at `LOCAL_CANDIDATE_HARD_CAP = 1000`, the function still ran `evaluateKeywordCandidate` (in `server/keyword-intelligence/rules.ts`) on every candidate. That evaluator scans pageMap × secondary keywords per call (~250 string comparisons each) and runs token-overlap, business-fit, and near-duplicate heuristics. On Swish (~235 tracked keywords + ~50 pages × multiple keywords + per-base variants × markets), the summary took 35.8 seconds — observed in Render logs as `totalMs: 35838`, `finalHeapMb: 101`. The badge fix re-introduced the exact hot-path regression that PRs #871–#873 had removed. This revert returns `localCandidates: 0` in the skinny summary path until a follow-up lands the proper fix. Test updated to lock in the 0 expectation so the regression cannot reappear silently.

items.title: Distinguish active-tracked-with-signal from active-tracked-awaiting-data
items.source: Live data hygiene audit on Swish 2026-05-21
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. Live audit found 175/235 (74%) of Swish active-tracked rows had `metrics.currentPosition == null` AND no GSC clicks/impressions — they were tracked in name only because the rank snapshot worker only writes rows for keywords that actually surface in GSC results. Showing all of them as plain 'Active' overstated tracking coverage. Fix: added `tracking.hasSignal: boolean` to the public `KeywordCommandCenterTrackingState` contract, set in `finalizeDraftRow` based on `row.metrics.currentPosition || clicks || impressions`. Drawer UI now renders 'Active · Awaiting data' with an amber sub-line ('No GSC clicks, impressions, or rank position recorded yet for this keyword.') when status is active and hasSignal is false. Also cleaned up the source detail rendering so residual `unknown` enum values don't display literally — show 'Source not recorded' instead. Field is omitted (undefined) for not_tracked rows since the signal concept doesn't apply. Two regression tests cover the true/false states.

items.title: Keyword Command Center tracking-source provenance recovery
items.source: Live data hygiene audit on Swish 2026-05-21
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. Live audit of Swish found 231/235 active-tracked rows had `tracking.source: "unknown"` — a legacy migration artifact since `rank-tracking.ts:140` defaults to UNKNOWN when source isn't supplied and `db/migrate-json.ts:1026` migrated pre-source-field tracked keywords without provenance. Effects: `protectedReason()` couldn't flag CLIENT_REQUESTED / MANUAL keywords as protected (234/235 unprotected on Swish), `trackedKeywordMatchesFilter(IN_STRATEGY)` missed inferred-strategy keywords, and the UI rendered the literal `unknown` string as a meaningful source detail. Fix: added `inferTrackedKeywordSources` in keyword-command-center.ts that cross-references the workspace's current strategy (siteKeywordMetrics → STRATEGY_PRIMARY, siteKeywords → STRATEGY_SITE_KEYWORD), keyword_feedback (requested → CLIENT_REQUESTED), and content_gaps (CONTENT_GAP) to upgrade UNKNOWN tracked keywords at read time. Applied at bundle assembly level so all downstream consumers see the inferred sources uniformly. Read-time only — does not persist back to storage so the inference can adapt as strategy evolves. UI detail-label code (`trackingSourceDetail`) now returns undefined for UNKNOWN so the literal string is never displayed. Six regression tests cover: each inference rule (strategy_primary, strategy_site_keyword, client_requested), unchanged-when-no-hint, recorded-source-wins-over-inference, and summary/rows alignment after inference.

items.title: Cheap localCandidates count for Keyword Command Center summary
items.notes: Completed in PR #G on 2026-05-21. Added `countLocalSeoKeywordCandidates(workspaceId)` in server/local-seo.ts that mirrors the candidate generator's outer iteration and cheap filters (declined, inactive, intent regex, market modifier regex) but skips `evaluateKeywordCandidate` — the per-candidate scan that caused the 35-second regression in PR #876. Sub-100ms even on Swish-scale workspaces. Slight overcount is possible (no eligibility evaluation) but acceptable as a UX hint — the actual displayable list still comes from the full generator when the user opens the Local Candidates filter. Three regression tests cover the cheap-path activation, the no-markets short-circuit (returns 0), and the LOCAL_CANDIDATE_HARD_CAP guardrail (count never exceeds 1000).

items.title: Keyword Command Center data hygiene polish
items.source: Live data hygiene audit on Swish 2026-05-21

(1) Assignment-gap UI: in-strategy rows lacking an assignment now render the inline column label in amber and show a dedicated drawer block explaining the keyword is in strategy but not yet mapped to a page. (2) Promotion CTA for untracked-with-rank rows: verified already working — needs_review + untracked rows show [add_to_strategy, track, decline] as next actions via existing buildNextActions logic (line 400-409 of keyword-command-center.ts). No code change needed; confirmed live on Swish. UI surfaces an amber 'X missing demand' badge in the panel header when missingVolume > 5% of the universe. The audit's 100% 'selected' observation reflects natural data distribution on Swish (most checked-local keywords are also in_strategy), not a bug.

items.title: Mask DataForSEO planner-bucket sentinel volumes in Keyword Command Center
items.source: Live data hygiene audit on Swish 2026-05-21
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. Live audit of the Swish workspace (371 rows) revealed 15 rows (~4% of universe, ~5.5% of active-tracked) displayed `volume: 1,000,000, difficulty: 21` — DataForSEO's Google Ads planner-grouped bucket sentinel pair. Branded long-tail keywords like 'contact swish dental' showed 1.0M monthly searches when the real value is well under 100. The codebase already has `isSuspiciousPlannerGroupedVolume` in `server/keyword-strategy-helpers.ts` and uses it in strategy generation paths, but it was never plugged into `keyword-command-center.ts` so the sentinel propagated through `mergeMetrics` and overwrote real provider data from page-keyword sources (later spreads win in the merge). Fix: wire the helper into `mergeMetrics` so the sentinel pair is dropped before merge. UI now renders `—` for affected rows instead of misleading 1.0M. The mask treats ALL volumes >= 1,000,000 as suspicious by design (rare for SMB workspaces to have legitimate million-volume keywords); if a niche ever needs higher legitimate ceilings, raise `PLANNER_GROUPED_VOLUME_FLOOR` in lockstep. Three regression tests cover: (a) sentinel from one source masked while real data from another wins, (b) orphan sentinel renders `—`, (c) sub-threshold real volumes preserved.

items.title: Local SEO visibility intelligence slice
items.source: Keyword Command Center stability and local SEO performance discussion 2026-05-21
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. New `LocalSeoSlice` wired through `buildWorkspaceIntelligence({ slices: ['localSeo'] })` so AdminChat, content/recommendation generation, and MCP `get_workspace_intelligence` are no longer blind to local SEO context. Design split: `candidates` carries the FULL bounded universe (capped only by LOCAL_CANDIDATE_HARD_CAP=1000 in local-seo.ts) so MCP consumers see everything; `effectiveLocalSeoBlock` is a pre-formatted, stratified-sampled prompt block (top 8 per active market, capped at 50 total) so AI prompts stay within token budget on hyper-local workspaces. Content generation has access to `selectRelevantLocalCandidates(slice, target, limit=15)` which boosts token-overlap and market-match candidates above unrelated higher-score ones — so per-piece content gen surfaces only locally relevant context. AdminChat includes the slice on performance/general questions and when the question mentions local signals (local, near me, GBP, market, location, city). Content + recommendation generation gate inclusion on a cheap active-markets check so non-local workspaces pay zero token cost. Empty-but-valid baseline returned when feature flag is off or no markets are configured. TODO documented in slice file: LocalSeoKeywordCandidate does not yet carry a `marketId` field, so per-market stratification falls back to flat top-N until that lands. No publishing, metadata writes, schema writes, GBP mutation, or strategy regeneration.

items.title: Local SEO refresh budget — default 100, per-workspace override 25–300

items.title: Local SEO candidate architecture — Tier 2 cheap/Evaluated split + TTL cache
items.source: Strategy refresh OOM crash + KCC Local Candidates 43s wait on Swish 2026-05-21
items.notes: Completed in this PR on 2026-05-21. Tier 2 refactor of `buildLocalSeoKeywordCandidates`. The old function ran the evaluator + suppression per candidate (scanning pageMap × secondaries × markets) and caused two production issues: (a) Strategy 'Refresh' button crashed staging via OOM kill, (b) KCC Local Candidates filter took 35–43s on Swish. Refactor: (1) Extracted `iterateLocalCandidateSignals` generator that yields source signals (explicit, strategy, tracking, page map, content gaps) without scoring or evaluation. (2) Split into two functions — `buildLocalSeoKeywordCandidates` is now the cheap default (no evaluator, empty reasons, no scoreDelta — safe for all current production callers: refresh path, KCC row enrichment, intelligence slice) and `buildLocalSeoKeywordCandidatesEvaluated` is the opt-in slow variant (preserves original evaluator+suppression+reasons). (3) `loadCandidateIterationContext` accepts `{ withEvaluationContext: true }` so the Evaluated path reuses the single `buildCandidateContext` read instead of calling it twice. (4) `countLocalSeoKeywordCandidates` rewritten to share the new generator. (5) No module-level TTL cache — an earlier draft included a 60s memo but it risked stale-data bugs after workspace mutations (admin declines a candidate but it reappears for 60s) and unbounded memory growth across explicitKeywords permutations; the cheap generator is fast enough on its own. pr-check rule retargeted to the Evaluated variant with new `local-candidates-evaluated-ok` hatch token. Scales for upcoming geo-grid tracking, local SEO recommendations, Google reviews, and client local SEO module — all of which need cheap candidate enumeration, not the evaluator. Includes regression tests for cheap-path reasons[], Evaluated-as-subset-of-cheap contract, Evaluated path reasons populated, and KCC Local Candidates <2s timing on a 30-page workspace.

items.title: Per-market keyword relevance — keyword↔market assignment model
items.notes: Today the refresh planner pairs every selected keyword with every active market — `dentist at houston` gets queried against Austin's market and surfaces Houston competitors as 'top local results' in the Austin context. Confirmed on Swish staging refresh 2026-05-21 (55 of 200 snapshots = 27.5% cross-market noise). Fix is non-trivial because it requires a keyword↔market assignment model. (1) New `keyword_markets` join table mapping which keywords should be checked against which markets. (2) Default: keyword applies to all active markets (matches current implicit behavior, no migration of existing data needed). (3) Override path: admin pins a keyword to specific market(s). (4) Auto-inference: if keyword text contains an active market's city/region name, default assignment to that market only. (5) Iterator + refresh planner respect the assignment. (6) UI: per-market detail blocks in keyword drawer instead of single block, with pin/unpin controls. (7) **Per-market volume/clicks/impressions reporting**: the keyword detail surface should show separate volume, GSC clicks, GSC impressions, and CTR per active market the keyword applies to — today these are workspace-global and don't distinguish between Austin and Houston performance for the same keyword. Requires per-market metric storage AND geo-targeted DataForSEO volume queries (see intel-quality-keyword-volume-geo-targeting). Deferred to P2 because no current client uses multi-market in production — Swish is the only multi-market workspace and is internal.

items.notes: Per-source-page 20% budget cap via applySourcePageCap. Explicit keywords bypass cap.

items.title: Google Business Profile health
items.notes: Follow-on after local-pack visibility v1. Audit and implement Google Business Profile/profile-health intelligence only when provider/API access is confirmed. Candidate signals include profile completeness, primary/secondary categories, services/products, photos, posts, opening hours, website/call/direction/bookings actions, profile views, and query discovery where available. Keep claims source-backed and do not imply GBP ownership unless OAuth/provider identity is verified.

items.title: Google reviews and local reputation intelligence
items.notes: Follow-on after local-pack visibility v1. Use Google Reviews or GBP-backed review sources only with explicit provider/cost guardrails. Candidate signals include rating, review count, review velocity, review themes, recent negative review posture, response opportunities, and competitor review gaps. This should feed local SEO reporting/recommendations and content/trust next actions, not auto-post reviews or responses.

items.title: Local geo-grid rank tracking
items.notes: Follow-on after local-pack visibility v1 and cost validation. Add coordinate-point/geographic grid tracking for local map visibility with strict caps, scheduling, cache/telemetry, and UI designed for local businesses. Use DataForSEO Maps/SERP capabilities or another provider only after modeling cost by keyword x coordinate x device x cadence. Keep geo-grid separate from GSC Rank Tracker snapshots and avoid auto-inferred grids from keywords alone.

items.title: Local SEO recommendations and action loop
items.notes: Follow-on after local visibility, GBP/review posture, and local reporting have stable data. Generate local SEO recommendations that combine local-pack visibility, workspace local posture, page/service-area coverage, LocalBusiness schema evidence, reviews/GBP signals where available, local competitors, keyword lifecycle, and outcomes such as calls/forms/bookings/directions. Recommendations must be source-backed, conservative, and route to safe next actions rather than publishing or mutating live sites.

items.title: Client-facing Local SEO dashboard and advisor rollout
items.notes:
Deferred until admin staging QA proves local visibility data quality and copy clarity. Public/client surfaces currently expose only legacy `local_pack` SERP-feature chips, not LocalSeoSlice data. Rollout should add a scrubbed client-safe local SEO summary to `/api/public/intelligence/:workspaceId`, the client Overview/Strategy surfaces, public AI advisor context, and briefing/recommendation surfaces only after conservative copy and tier gating are approved. Do not expose raw provider evidence, match internals, admin-only candidate state, or claim verified local rank unless the evidence explicitly supports it. Guardrails added on 2026-05-24 to pin the deferred boundary: public workspace bootstrap, public intelligence, and public strategy responses must not expose LocalSeoSlice, local visibility snapshots, candidate state, service/repeat-competitor internals, or prompt-only local SEO blocks until this rollout item is intentionally implemented.
[Platform health recommendation pass 2026-05-26: promoted priority because client surfaces still rely on legacy local_pack signaling while LocalSeoSlice is admin-only.]

items.title: Local SEO intelligence layer for service-area businesses
items.source: Staging QA and keyword operating-loop discussion 2026-05-19; decomposed by PR14 audit 2026-05-20
items.notes: Superseded and decomposed by the PR14 local SEO foundation audit. The original broad local SEO intelligence-layer concept is now split into execution-ready roadmap items: `intel-quality-local-pack-visibility-foundation`, `intel-quality-local-seo-reporting-strategy-integration`, `intel-quality-google-business-profile-health`, `intel-quality-google-reviews-local-reputation`, `intel-quality-local-geo-grid-tracking`, and `intel-quality-local-seo-recommendations`. Keeping this item marked done prevents duplicate roadmap selection while preserving the original intent: local SEO should remain posture-aware, optional for non-local workspaces, conservative about match confidence, and integrated through the keyword/intelligence operating loop rather than as one-off city-modifier logic.

items.title: MCP actions phases 3+4 integration verification closeout
items.notes: Completed on 2026-05-25. Closed out MCP actions with end-to-end integration coverage for all 10 tools (`tests/integration/mcp-tools-{keyword,content,jobs}.test.ts`), corrected send-to-client activity typing to `brief_sent_for_review` / `post_sent_for_review`, removed the stale `brief:updated` pending-routes allowlist entry now that MCP save_brief broadcasts it, and fixed post send-to-client request lifecycle typing so creation supports `in_progress` before transitioning to `post_review`. Final verification gate passed: typecheck, pr-check, targeted MCP test suites, and full suite/build in this phase.

items.title: MCP capability upgrade — reliability + top-5 operator surface
items.notes: Completed on 2026-05-31. Implemented the top-5 MCP capability expansion with reliability-first sequencing: (1) hardened MCP JSON-schema conversion for union/object edge cases and moved edit/update schemas to object-first contracts, (2) expanded `send_to_client` to support by-ID targets (`brief_id` / `post_id`) while keeping handle flows, (3) added strict local SEO refresh MCP input validation, (4) surfaced content-request tools (`list_content_requests`, `get_content_request`, `create_content_request`), (5) surfaced job observability tools (`get_job_status`, `list_jobs`, `cancel_job`), and (6) surfaced keyword strategy CRUD tools (`get_keyword_strategy`, `remove_page_keyword`, `add_keywords_batch`, `replace_keyword_strategy`). Added/updated unit, integration, and contract tests including a guard that MCP object input schemas always provide top-level `properties`.

items.title: MCP limitations Sprint 2 core closeout
items.notes: Completed on 2026-05-31. Shipped Sprint 2 core MCP gaps in four slices: workspace lifecycle tools (`create_workspace`, `update_workspace` moderate allowlist, `delete_workspace`) plus `get_workspace_overview` enrichments (`effective_tier`, `client_portal_url`); content/post lifecycle tools (`get_content_performance`, `delete_brief`, `delete_post`, `list_post_versions`, `revert_post_version`); insights/intelligence enrichments (`get_unresolved_insights`, `get_insights` optional `domain`, `get_keyword_analysis.discovered_query_summary`, and `get_workspace_intelligence` enrichment flags for backlinks/entity-resolution/site-inventory); and MCP-layer filters for `list_briefs` / `list_posts` (`status`, `page_type`). Added unit/integration coverage for new contracts and branches while preserving `source: mcp-chat` provenance, broadcast-on-write behavior, and additive backward-compatible contracts.

items.title: MCP content edit orchestration (server-side iterative sessions)
items.notes: Deferred follow-up after MCP read/write iterative editing v1. Evaluate optional server-managed edit orchestration only if operational pain justifies it (high conflict rates, auditability gaps, repeated failed applies). Scope may include edit-session lifecycle, staged deltas, and optional server AI wrapper endpoints while preserving existing service-layer writes, activity provenance (`source: mcp-chat`), and broadcast invalidation contracts.

items.title: E-E-A-T Asset Library — Personalized Trust Signal Recommendations
items.notes: Completed 2026-05-27 on `codex/schema-intel-final-review`. Added `eeat_assets` workspace storage (migration 105 + CRUD routes/store + admin settings tab), intelligence slice registration (`eeatAssets`) with prompt formatter support, and workspace/event/query-key wiring (`eeat-assets:updated`). Added deterministic trust-signal evaluation (`server/eeat-trust-signals.ts`) and threaded it into both single-page and bulk page analysis persistence so `page_keywords` now stores `missingTrustSignals` + `eeatAssetRecommendations` and clears them on re-analysis resets. Content brief generation now injects specific E-E-A-T asset recommendations (page-level first, workspace fallback) instead of generic trust guidance. Schema generation now receives workspace E-E-A-T inventory through schema context and enriches Article author structured data with job title, identity links, and credential nodes when available. Added guardrail documentation at `docs/rules/eeat-asset-library.md`. Follow-up shipped 2026-05-28: Brand & AI E-E-A-T tab now supports one-click auto-fill from existing Page Intelligence trust gaps and business-profile/schema context (`POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/eeat-assets/autofill`) to seed inventory with deterministic starter assets.

items.title: Platform UX Audit 2026-05-19 — Quick Wins Batch
items.notes:
Target easiest high-impact fixes from the 54-finding platform UX audit without deep refactors.
Initial quick-win scope:
1) P0-6: Replace client ROI error copy to remove vendor references + add retry/next-step CTA.
2) P0-7: Map internal Inbox action source enums to client-safe labels.
3) P0-8/P3-10: Replace remaining hard-coded route strings with adminPath()/clientPath() helpers.
4) P1-10: HealthTab no-audit raw div -> <EmptyState> with actionable CTA.
5) P2-8: Replace generic "Loading..." fallbacks in ContentPipeline with contextual LoadingState copy.
6) P2-13: Rename SearchTab "Raw Data" label to client-friendly wording.
7) P2-9: Onboarding wizard CTA uses legacy tab alias content -> reviews deep-link.
8) P3-11: Replace GA4 jargon in client Analytics tab labels.
Preserve tab deep-link two-halves contract and run pr-check + typecheck + build after patch set.
[Completed 2026-05-25 on codex/platform-ux-quickwins] Delivered the initial quick-win batch: removed vendor-facing ROI error copy, added retry CTA, mapped client action source badges to client-safe labels, replaced hard-coded admin route strings with adminPath(), converted HealthTab fallback to EmptyState + CTA, replaced generic ContentPipeline loading copy, renamed SearchTab Raw Data label, updated onboarding routing away from legacy content alias, and replaced GA4 jargon labels in client analytics.
[Batch 2 follow-up 2026-05-25] Added explicit no-data fallback for Performance when both GSC+GA4 are unavailable, included copy-review availability in the Inbox nav badge count, replaced dynamic `grid-cols-${n}` construction with static responsive classes in Overview metrics, made Search Health Summary responsive on mobile, deep-linked Overview Generate Brief CTA to `/inbox?tab=reviews`, and redirected unknown admin tabs back to workspace Home instead of rendering a blank pane.
[Batch 3 follow-up 2026-05-25] Replaced SEO Audit silent blank fallback with actionable ErrorState, surfaced active Inbox loading state from existing approvals/requests loading props, added Content Plan review-badge deep-link to Inbox Reviews, added admin toast confirmation/failure feedback for Content Brief "Send to client", added "Show welcome tour" entrypoint in the client header, upgraded Strategy no-data empty state with Inbox CTA, surfaced client copy suggestions in the admin Copy Review panel, and added annotation create/update/delete workspace broadcasts on both annotation route surfaces for real-time refresh.
[Batch 4 follow-up 2026-05-25] Added Meeting Brief source-tab drill-ins (metrics, wins, attention, recommendations, blueprint), made Outcomes Overview rows open per-workspace Action Results with keyboard support and added a non-blank feature-flag-off fallback, wired Rank Tracker handoffs to Page Intelligence plus Strategy CTA from empty state, and standardized contextual LoadingState/ErrorState handling across RankTracker, PageIntelligence, ContentBriefs, InternalLinks, and WorkspaceHome.

items.notes: Optional polish: codeBlocks → CodeRepository/SoftwareSourceCode (low ROI for agency sites); headings → WebPage.speakable cssSelector (overlaps with schema-engagement-signals; whichever ships first wins). May be skipped if cycles are short.

items.title: Remove dead pageKeywordMap from buildSchemaContext + generateSchemaSuggestions signature
items.notes: Completed 2026-05-24 on `codex/roadmap-quick-wins` as a staging reconciliation. The dead `pageKeywordMap` return field, caller destructures, schema-suggester parameter, and void discard were already removed on `origin/staging`; `generateSchemaSuggestions()` now reads per-page keywords through `buildWorkspaceIntelligence()` inside the page loop. This item is closed as roadmap cleanup with no additional production code changes needed.

items.title: Schema entity grounding Phase A — shared contracts + guardrails
items.notes: Completed 2026-05-27 on `codex/schema-intel-prA-contract-guardrails`. Added `shared/types/entity-resolution.ts` typed contracts (Thing/Place entities, Wikidata references, availability states), added `docs/rules/schema-entity-resolution.md` as the feature guardrail document, and added pr-check custom rule `Wikidata disambiguation outside entity-resolution intelligence modules` with fixture coverage in `tests/pr-check.test.ts`. This phase intentionally ships no runtime schema behavior change; implementation wiring remains tracked under `schema-entity-grounding-wikidata` and `schema-intelligence-layer-v2`.

items.title: Schema entity grounding Phase B — intelligence slice wiring
items.notes: Completed 2026-05-27 on `codex/schema-intel-prB-slice-wiring`. Added `entityResolution` to the shared intelligence slice registry and workspace intelligence assembler routing, added `server/intelligence/entity-resolution-slice.ts` with deterministic Thing/Place candidate assembly from workspace/page strategy context, and extended `buildSchemaIntelligence()` options/results to expose optional `entityResolution` reads. Added unit coverage for the new slice and schema-intelligence wrapper wiring. No schema template emission changes in this phase.

items.title: Schema entity grounding Phase C — Wikidata resolution + cache
items.notes: Completed 2026-05-27 on `codex/schema-intel-prC-wikidata-resolution`. Added `entity_resolution_cache` persistence (migration 104 + `server/intelligence/entity-resolution-cache.ts`), added bounded SPARQL disambiguation with confidence scoring in `server/intelligence/entity-resolution-wikidata.ts`, and upgraded `assembleEntityResolution()` to merge resolved Wikidata references while preserving deterministic fallbacks and degraded availability on lookup failures. Added `IntelligenceOptions.resolveEntityReferences` opt-in and threaded schema-intelligence calls to enable live resolution only for explicit entity-resolution consumers.

items.title: Schema entity grounding Phase D — typed entity emission wiring
items.notes: Completed 2026-05-27 on `codex/schema-intel-prD-template-emission`. Threaded entity-resolution intelligence into schema-suggester generation calls (`includeEntityResolution: true` + per-page surface mapping), extended schema data-source contracts for typed entity payloads, and upgraded homepage/article/service/local-business templates to emit typed Thing/Place nodes for knowsAbout/about/mentions/areaServed with Wikidata `sameAs` when available. Organization sameAs now merges social profiles with entity-derived references. Added template and source-contract coverage for threading and emission behavior.

items.title: Schema engagement signals — wordCount, timeRequired, speakable, educationalLevel
items.notes: Article.wordCount shipped in the Blog/Article Schema Quality Hardening pass (May 2026) using visible content extraction instead of full articleBody duplication. Remaining quick-win Tier-4 fields with data the platform already has: Article.timeRequired = wordCount/250wpm rounded; WebPage.speakable with cssSelector pointing to .lead/.intro/first-paragraph — voice-assistant rich result; Article.educationalLevel via AI classification (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced). All wire through existing PageData typed boundary. No new admin UI required for the basics; speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant. Independent rich-result eligibility unlock for Google Article + voice-search amplification.

items.title: Schema E-E-A-T amplifiers — publishingPrinciples, reviewedBy, copyrightHolder, isAccessibleForFree, Person.jobTitle
items.notes: Tier-3 trust signals Google increasingly weighs. Add 4 new optional Workspace fields: publishingPrinciplesUrl, actionableFeedbackPolicyUrl (both → Organization.publishingPrinciples + actionableFeedbackPolicy on every page's @graph). Article.copyrightHolder ref to Organization, copyrightYear from datePublished. Article.reviewedBy as Person from new CMS field 'fact_checker' (optional). Article.isAccessibleForFree from per-tier content gating. Person.jobTitle + worksFor on author when CMS author records have those fields (currently they're just string names). Mostly admin Settings UI work + template emission. Each field is an additive E-E-A-T signal — non-breaking, immediate ranking benefit on YMYL content.

items.title: Schema entity grounding — typed Thing/Place entities + Wikidata sameAs disambiguation
items.notes:
Tier-2 / biggest competitive moat. Replace string knowsAbout (currently ['brand strategy','webflow']) with typed Thing entities linked to Wikidata Q-numbers via sameAs. Same upgrade for Article.about (typed Thing — what is this article about?), Article.mentions[] (entities referenced in body), Place entities for areaServed (Austin, TX → Wikidata Q16559), Organization.sameAs extended with Wikidata identifier. Architecture: new EntityResolutionSlice on buildWorkspaceIntelligence, populated by a Cheerio-based extractor that runs entity recognition (likely via OpenAI structured-output prompt against curated entity types) + Wikidata SPARQL lookup for top candidates, cached per-entity. Most agencies don't do this; Google's knowledge graph rewards disambiguation heavily. Architectural prerequisite met by parity-fields PR1 Pattern B starter. Can ship after schema-page-element-catalog-v1 (the catalog feeds Article.mentions extraction).
[Platform health recommendation pass 2026-05-26: prioritized as the next emergent intelligence slice (EntityResolutionSlice) to prevent schema authority drift and duplicate ad hoc entity handling.]
[Phase A completed 2026-05-27: contracts + guardrails shipped in roadmap item `schema-entity-grounding-phase-a-contract-guardrails`.]
[Phase B completed 2026-05-27: intelligence slice scaffolding + schema-intelligence wrapper wiring shipped in roadmap item `schema-entity-grounding-phase-b-slice-wiring`.]
[Phase C completed 2026-05-27: Wikidata disambiguation + cache shipped in roadmap item `schema-entity-grounding-phase-c-wikidata-resolution`.]
[Phase D completed 2026-05-27: typed schema emission wiring shipped in roadmap item `schema-entity-grounding-phase-d-template-emission`; remaining follow-up is confidence tuning/telemetry.]

Now complete: foundational phases A-D are shipped (contract guardrails, slice wiring, Wikidata resolution/cache, typed schema emission). Remaining confidence tuning/telemetry is follow-up quality work, not a blocker for this roadmap item.

items.title: Schema commerce types — Service.offers (PriceSpecification), Product, Course, Event
items.notes: Tier-5 monetization opportunity for agencies with productized services / paid content. Service.offers with Offer { price, priceCurrency, availability } — Google's 'service prices' SERP feature. Product schema for templates/digital products. Course for educational content/training. Event for webinars/launches with eventStatus + eventAttendanceMode (Online/Offline/Mixed). Each is independent rich-result eligibility unlock. Workspace.contentPricing already exists (used for content-purchase tiers); Service.offers can reuse the pricing infra. Per-type effort similar to parity-fields PR1's per-template additions; parallelizable across template files. Default-off; admin opts in per-workspace. Most useful for agencies with packaged services (web-design + brand audit + content retainer); skip for service-only sites without published prices.

items.title: Schema trust/authority — Organization.award, member, hasCredential, numberOfEmployees
items.notes: Tier-6 reputation graph fields. New Workspace.organizationAwards (string[] of Award names → Award entity[]), Workspace.organizationMemberships (industry orgs → ProgramMembership/Organization references), Workspace.organizationCredentials (certifications → EducationalOccupationalCredential), Workspace.numberOfEmployees (QuantitativeValue { minValue, maxValue, unitText: 'employee' }). Each emitted on Organization sibling node in homepage + local-business templates. Pure admin Settings UI + template extension; no AI/intelligence dependencies. Agency credibility in SERP, especially for B2B competitive queries.

items.title: Schema Intelligence Layer (FAQ-from-GSC, E-E-A-T, healthcare subtypes)
items.notes: Follow-up to lean-schema-rewrite. Re-injects the platform's intelligence layer surgically into schema generation after the current deterministic schema path is stable: (1) Healthcare type escalation through canonical seoContext/business profile signals, falling back to LocalBusiness only when evidence supports it. (2) FAQ enrichment from GSC question queries via a new slice-backed helper if this feature is revived; the old unused extractFaqOpportunities/schema prompt block was removed in Workspace Intelligence Consolidation PR4. (3) E-E-A-T author injection from content brief evidence into Article.author with Person.knowsAbout/sameAs. (4) Service serviceType from voice profile + business profile. Any revived intelligence enrichment must flow through buildSchemaIntelligence() or another documented central intelligence builder, not direct raw workspace reads.

items.title: Schema Workstream C — Entity Graph Hub Dispatch
items.notes: Workstream C shipped: hub pages now emit semantically correct schema types with cross-page @id references via assembleSiteContext + SiteContext plumbing into generateLeanSchema. BlogIndex → Blog with blogPost[] (@id refs, sorted newest-first, capped at 10, numberOfItems = true count). ServiceIndex → Service + hasOfferCatalog: { @type: OfferCatalog, hasPart[] }. items.title: Schema Semantic Extraction & AI-Powered Generation
items.notes: AI-powered universal page content extraction layer for schema generation. extractSemanticData() (Haiku 4.5 tool_use) extracts phone, address, hours, staff, services, aggregateRating, sameAs, faq, primaryImage per page — stored on PageElementCatalog.semantics. applyPostEnrichment() appends FAQPage/AggregateRating/sameAs/VideoObject nodes to generated schema using extracted semantics with rollback-on-new-errors safety. generateSchemaForUnknownType() generates WebPage-classified pages with non-empty semantics via second Haiku call + static SCHEMA_TYPE_REFERENCE. Validator extended with DEDICATED_VALIDATOR_TYPES guard, unverified-type warnings for unknown Schema.org types, addressCountry in ContactPage. GSC URL Inspection integration: inspectUrlForRichResults() calls GSC API 3 minutes after publish, stores google_validation_status on schema_publish_history. schema.org pre-publish validator: validateWithSchemaOrg() fires at generation time (fire-and-forget, logs only). Migrations 081 (google_validation_status on schema_publish_history) + 082 (schema_org_validation_status on schema_snapshots). WS_EVENTS.SCHEMA_GOOGLE_VALIDATION added. 9 new/modified test files; 5805 tests passing.

items.title: Schema Pillar 2.1 — Stop emitting SearchAction on sites without search
items.notes: Pillar 2's homepage + LocalBusiness templates emitted `potentialAction: SearchAction` unconditionally. Google's docs require the site actually expose a working search endpoint at the urlTemplate; emitting it on a site without search misrepresents capability. Fix: dropped SearchAction emission until a workspace-level signal indicates site search is available. Re-add as opt-in via Workspace.siteHasSearch (or auto-detect from homepage HTML <form action=/search>) is queued under schema-yoast-parity-fields.

items.title: Schema Pillar 1 — Raise the Validator Bar
items.notes: REQUIRED_BY_TYPE in validator.ts now enforces Yoast/Google-baseline completeness. Article/BlogPosting require image+dateModified+articleSection+isPartOf+breadcrumb+inLanguage. WebPage variants require description+isPartOf+breadcrumb+inLanguage. LocalBusiness requires address+telephone+inLanguage. WebSite requires inLanguage (potentialAction is conditional, deferred to schema-yoast-parity-fields where it gates on Workspace.siteHasSearch). Organization requires logo. Cross-ref validators reject malformed isPartOf/breadcrumb shapes and dangling BreadcrumbList @id pointers. NEW value-shape validators reject malformed Article author/publisher/image, non-ISO 8601 dates, non-contiguous breadcrumb positions, and non-absolute URLs on primary nodes. Locks the bar Pillars 2 + 2.1 raised. 24 new test assertions across required-fields, cross-ref shape, and value-shape describe blocks.
