sprints.rationale: Close the drift and duplication findings surfaced by the 2026-05-27 four-agent platform audit. Highest-priority items are two unauthenticated public endpoints and a set of admin/client divergence bugs where the same concept is computed twice with different formulas. Bulk of the sprint is mechanical adoption sweeps to migrate ~150 frontend call sites onto existing shared primitives (scoreColor, fmtNum, fmtMoney, useToggleSet, plus new formatBytes/formatDate helpers) and lock each sweep in with a pr-check rule so the drift cannot reopen.

items.title: Add portal auth to public rank-tracking and audit-traffic endpoints
items.notes: Security finding. `server/routes/rank-tracking.ts:125,133` and `server/routes/public-portal.ts:310` (audit-traffic) lack `requireClientPortalAuth`. Admin equivalents (`rank-tracking.ts:112,120`) use `requireWorkspaceAccess`. Ranking data and GA/GSC page metrics currently leak to anyone with a known workspaceId. [Expanded 2026-05-27 during plan verification] `server/routes/anomalies.ts:27` (public GET) also lacks auth, and admin mutations `anomalies.ts:34,43` (dismiss/acknowledge) have no workspace guard at all. Bundle all six fixes plus a pr-check rule that flags new routes mounted on the `/api/public/` prefix without an explicit auth middleware or a documented `// public-no-auth-ok` hatch. [DONE 2026-05-27 — Plan A: added requireAuthenticatedClientPortalAuth to rank-tracking (4 routes), public-portal audit-traffic, anomalies public GET + admin mutations; added pr-check rule Public route under /api/public/ missing client-portal auth middleware.]

items.title: Sweep remaining /api/public/ routes onto requireAuthenticatedClientPortalAuth + migrate tests
items.notes: While implementing the P0 endpoint-auth fix we found ~19 additional /api/public/<resource>/:workspaceId routes that rely on the global app gate (server/app.ts:262) without per-route portal auth. The global gate short-circuits on workspaces with no clientPassword, so each of these still leaks data during the pre-setup window. Files grandfathered in the pr-check rule's exclude list: public-portal.ts (12 routes), public-content.ts (mutations beyond the file-level router.use covered ones), public-requests.ts (6), recommendations.ts (2), reports.ts (3), stripe.ts (2), work-orders.ts (2). Migration is large because adding requireAuthenticatedClientPortalAuth() broke ~798 existing integration tests that seed workspaces without clientPassword and expect 200 on public endpoints. Sweep route-by-route, updating each integration-test file in the same PR to authenticate via /api/public/auth/:id before hitting data endpoints. When a file's routes are all migrated, remove that file from the pr-check rule's exclude list. Coordinate with the broader test-fixture refactor (consider a shared `authenticatePortal(ctx, wsId)` helper). [PARTIAL 2026-06-02 (PR #1035): work-orders.ts removed from the pr-check auth-sweep exclude (both its public GETs use requireClientPortalAuth; rule accepts either variant). ~18 unprotected /api/public/ routes still remain (public-portal.ts, reports.ts).] [PARTIAL 2026-06-03 (Task #13): recommendations.ts both public routes (GET list + POST .../generate) migrated to requireClientPortalAuth() — soft gate so passwordless/demo portals still load (the client useRecommendations/InsightsEngine hook fetches token-less); the // public-no-auth-ok inline hatches removed. recommendations.ts was never in the exclude array (it relied on inline hatches), so only the stale exclude-list comment was updated. Same PR also de-costed the GET: it no longer runs generateRecommendations inline on a cache-miss (returns last-known/empty set; 404 on unknown ws). Remaining unprotected: public-portal.ts, reports.ts.] [PARTIAL 2026-06-08 audit wave A/D: clientPortalEnabled is now centrally enforced in the public API gate plus requireClientPortalAuth/requireAuthenticatedClientPortalAuth; pre-auth login/session routes refuse disabled portals; public client mutations in public-content, recommendations, client-actions, content-plan review, keyword feedback/business-priorities/content-gap votes, business-profile, copy review, upgrade checkout, ROI, and workspace report list now require a real authenticated client/admin actor. Passwordless public reads remain supported where intentionally read-only. Remaining sweep follow-up: split unauthenticated workspace bootstrap metadata from authenticated client workspace payload and finish any lower-risk public read route classification.]

items.title: P3 residual consolidation
items.notes: Completed 2026-06-04. Retired the legacy `server/db/json-column.ts` shim and removed its tests; all JSON parsing coverage now targets `server/db/json-validation.ts`. Collapsed server/frontend title-casing onto shared `capitalizeWord` helpers, migrated schema data-source and insight-feed title generation, collapsed keyword-strategy `compact()` siblings onto `compactStrings`, and promoted `sleep(ms)` from `server/local-seo.ts` into `server/helpers.ts`. Follow-up review also removed the clipboard-upload `sips` shellout in favor of existing `sharp` resizing after invalid test image bytes triggered native macOS `sips` crashes. Prior work had already completed `useDebouncedValue`, `daysSince`, and quickWins parity. Verification sweep now leaves no `parseJsonColumn`/`stringifyJsonColumn`, local `compact()`, local `sleep()`, `titleCaseWord`, or `capitalizeSlugSegment` callsites outside roadmap history.

items.title: Move schema-plan generation onto the durable job platform
items.source: Durable Jobs + Realtime Stability Plan — PR 2 (2026-06-08)
items.notes: Completed on 2026-06-08. Added `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.SCHEMA_PLAN_GENERATION`, `server/schema-plan-generation-job.ts`, and the shared `SCHEMA_PLAN_UPDATED` realtime event. `/api/jobs` now starts or attaches to schema-plan generation with duplicate-active protection, and the legacy `POST /api/webflow/schema-plan/:siteId` route is reduced to a compatibility wrapper that returns `{ jobId, deprecated: true }` instead of running AI inline. `SchemaPlanPanel` now reads the stored plan through React Query, starts regeneration through `useBackgroundTasks`, and refreshes the persisted plan on job completion. Schema-plan save/send/activate/delete/client-feedback paths now all emit the shared plan-update event so admin/client caches and intelligence consumers stay aligned. Added lifecycle coverage for `/api/jobs`, the legacy wrapper route, duplicate-active attachment, job metadata/contracts, and schema-plan websocket invalidation wiring.

items.title: Consolidate Brand & AI overview IA and business footprint surfaces
items.notes:
Brand & AI has become bulky and overlaps adjacent tabs. Consolidate IA without collapsing distinct data authorities. Keep separate models: `workspace.businessProfile` (schema/contact authority), `client_locations` (local-SEO operational authority), and `workspace.intelligenceProfile` (strategic AI context). UX target: Overview shows compact current snapshots + deep links (Current Context, Intelligence Profile, Business Profile, Locations), and the editing flow groups Business Profile + Locations into one business-footprint editing surface to reduce navigation churn. Done when: no duplicate long-form editors remain in Overview, legacy deep links continue to resolve, and Locations + Business Profile remain separately persisted but co-located in admin UX.

Completed 2026-06-05. Brand Hub now uses `business-footprint` as the canonical tab while preserving `business-profile` and `locations` as legacy aliases. Business Profile and Locations are co-located in one admin surface without merging their storage or route contracts, schema/local-SEO senders now point at the canonical tab, and Overview opens with compact summary cards for Current Context, Intelligence Profile, Business Profile, and Locations before the existing richer current-context sections.
